department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The department is recruiting for five tiered research positions (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on a 24-month contract to support a project investigating creep age forming technology for 7050 aluminum aircraft panels. Candidates must hold relevant doctoral or master's degrees depending on the position level, with experience requirements ranging from entry-level (honours degree for Research Assistant) to senior postdoctoral researchers (three+ years experience). The roles involve assisting the project leader with research activities on advanced materials processing technology applicable to aerospace manufacturing. Applicants should contact the project coordinator for additional information about the specific position and requirements.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This posting offers full-time or part-time positions at three levels—Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant—in the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering for a twelve-month appointment period. Appointees will support research into multimodal human-robot collaboration, focusing on few-shot perception and spatial skill learning with human-in-the-loop approaches. Required qualifications vary by position level: Postdoctoral Fellows need a doctoral degree with no more than five years post-qualification experience; Research Associates require a Master's degree or strong honours degree plus three or more years of relevant work experience; Research Assistants need an honours degree or equivalent. Competitive remuneration is offered for full-time positions.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: These full-time and part-time positions support research on vision-based human digital twin modelling and scene understanding for human-robot collaboration within the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering. Three appointment levels are available: Postdoctoral Fellow (requiring a doctoral degree with up to five years post-qualification experience), Research Associate (requiring a Master's degree or honours degree with three or more years of relevant experience), and Research Assistant (requiring an honours degree). All positions are twelve-month contracts offering competitive remuneration for full-time roles.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering is recruiting for full-time or part-time positions across three levels—Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant—to support a research project focused on developing ultrastrong, ductile, and thermally stable nanocrystalline alloys using novel nano-honeycomb architectural designs. Requirements vary by position level, ranging from a doctoral degree with minimal post-qualification experience for the Fellow role, to a master's or honours degree with three years of research experience for the Associate role, to an honours degree for the Assistant role. Appointments are for twelve months with competitive remuneration. Candidates should contact the project leader for further details.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position involves a 24-month appointment to support research on large language models applied to computational social science and fintech. Key responsibilities include identifying research challenges, collecting and processing data, developing machine learning frameworks, validating algorithms, and deploying language models on cloud servers. Candidates must hold a recent doctorate in information systems, computer science, applied mathematics, or statistics, with strong expertise in natural language processing and proficiency in Python, PyTorch, Linux, and Git. Strong communication skills in English and Chinese, combined with publications in deep learning or NLP, are essential, with preference given to those with high-quality peer-reviewed papers in these fields.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: Two positions are available in the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering to support research on high-Q microcavity-enhanced fluorescence spectroscopic optofluidic chips for detecting water pollutants. The Postdoctoral Fellow role (6-24 months) requires a doctoral degree in optical, electronic, or electrical engineering with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while the Research Associate position (3-12 months, full-time or part-time) requires a master's degree or strong honours degree with at least three years of relevant research experience. Both appointees will assist the project leader in developing advanced detection technologies for water quality applications. Interested candidates should contact the project supervisor for further information.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Logistics and Maritime Studies
description: The Department of Logistics and Maritime Studies seeks postdoctoral fellows, research associates, or research assistants (available on full-time or part-time bases for 6-12 months) to support a research project on optimization for logistics management. Candidates must demonstrate experience in optimization algorithm design and implementation, with qualifications ranging from honours degrees for research assistants to doctoral degrees for postdoctoral fellows, and varying levels of prior research experience depending on the position level. The roles involve assisting the project leader with research activities within the optimization and logistics domain. Interested applicants should contact the project supervisor for additional details.

title: Part-time Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: These are twelve-month part-time positions in industrial and systems engineering supporting research on rapid reconfiguration of human-robot collaborative assembly lines for smart manufacturing. Three appointment levels are available: Postdoctoral Fellow (requiring a doctoral degree with no more than five years post-qualification experience), Research Associate (requiring a Master's degree or strong honours degree with three or more years relevant experience), and Research Assistant (requiring an honours degree). Appointees will assist the project leader in developing methodologies to enable human-centric manufacturing systems that can quickly adapt robotic assembly configurations.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Building Environment and Energy Engineering
description: This twelve-month Research Assistant position involves supporting a policy research project examining Legal Entity Identifier and electronic authentication frameworks across Guangdong, Hong Kong, and Macao, including analysis of their effectiveness and societal responses. Candidates must hold an honours degree in Engineering or equivalent qualification. The role offers competitive remuneration and requires candidates to assist the project leader in conducting comprehensive policy research and stakeholder engagement across the three regions.

title: Research Assistant
description: This three-month research assistant position involves supporting a project on neuromorphic multi-paradigm learning that integrates biological plasticity mechanisms. Candidates must hold an honours degree and demonstrate strong technical competency in PyTorch programming, spiking neural networks, and familiarity with brain-inspired learning methods and large language models. The role is distinctive for its focus on bridging neuroscience and artificial intelligence through neuromorphic computing approaches. A competitive remuneration package is offered.

title: Research Assistant
description: This six-month research assistant position involves supporting the development of an autonomous kitchen assistant and robotic cooking system within the biomedical engineering department. Candidates must hold an honours degree in Computer Science or a related field and demonstrate proficiency in software engineering. The role offers competitive remuneration and appeals to those interested in robotics and automation applied to domestic environments.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate (Full-time/Part-time) / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: The Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering seeks to fill three related positions (Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on contracts ranging from three to twenty-four months. Appointees will support research on ultra-sensitive optical fiber-tip optomechanical sensors designed for gas sensing applications in electronic nose technology. Requirements vary by level: Postdoctoral Fellows need a doctoral degree with no more than five years post-qualification experience, Research Associates require a master's degree or strong honours degree in engineering with at least three years of relevant experience, and Research Assistants need a relevant honours degree. The role offers both full-time and part-time options with flexible contract lengths.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
department: Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: This posting offers three tiered research positions (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, and Research Associate) in the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering, each available for 3-24 months, to support a project investigating key technologies of complex antenna systems in the sub-10GHz band. The Research Fellow role requires a doctoral degree plus at least three years of postdoctoral experience, while the Postdoctoral Fellow position requires a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience. The Research Associate position requires a master's degree or strong honours degree with three or more years of relevant experience, plus demonstrated expertise in BLE system design, AI data analysis, and mechanical system design, along with strong English communication skills. All appointees will work under a project leader to advance antenna system research technologies.

title: Part-time Project Associate / Project Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This is an eight-month part-time position in the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering to support a project on engineering ethics for emerging technologies. The appointee will assist the project leader in executing research activities and organizing project-related events. The role requires either a Master's degree or strong honours degree with three or more years of research experience for the Associate level, or an honours degree for the Assistant level, with preference given to candidates with established connections to secondary schools. The hourly rates are HK$178 for Project Associates and HK$125 for Project Assistants.

title: Research Associate
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: This is a twelve-month Research Associate position in aeronautical and aviation engineering focused on assisting with a project on modelling and optimizing low-altitude transport systems for logistics and passenger applications. The role requires a doctoral degree or equivalent qualification. The position offers competitive remuneration and applicants should contact the listed project leader for additional information, with applications due by 15 May 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This seven-month position in Industrial and Systems Engineering involves assisting the project leader in research on methodologies for rapid reconfiguration of human-robot collaborative assembly production lines in smart manufacturing. The role is offered at three levels—Postdoctoral Fellow (requiring a doctorate and maximum five years post-qualification experience), Research Associate (requiring a Master's degree or strong honours degree with three or more years of research experience), or Research Assistant (requiring an honours degree). The position offers flexibility in qualifications and experience levels, allowing applicants to match their background to the appropriate role tier.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Project Associate / Project Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: Three fixed-term positions (twelve-month appointments) are available in the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering, offering full-time or part-time arrangements at three levels: Postdoctoral Fellow, Project Associate, and Project Assistant. All positions involve assisting with research on an indoor biomedical equipment locator system. Requirements vary by level: Postdoctoral Fellows must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years post-qualification experience; Project Associates need a Master's degree or strong honours degree plus three or more years of relevant research experience; Project Assistants require an honours degree or equivalent. Interested candidates should contact the supervising professor for further details.

title: Research Assistant
description: This is a six to twelve-month research assistant position in the Department of Computing focused on developing an AI-powered learning assistant system designed for flipped classroom environments. The role involves supporting the project leader with research and development work that requires hands-on experience in Unity or Web development, digital avatar creation, and large language model solutions. Candidates must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification and demonstrate genuine interest in human-computer interaction and e-learning research. A competitive salary package is offered, with applications closing on 5 May 2026.

title: Principal Research Fellow / Senior Research Fellow / Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant / Research Technical Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
description: The Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering seeks to fill multiple positions across seven levels—from Principal Research Fellow to Research Technical Assistant—to support research on smart railway technology and applications within a national research center's Hong Kong branch. Appointments are available on a full-time or part-time basis for six-month terms, with three posts at each level. Qualifications range from doctoral degrees with substantial postdoctoral experience (8+ years for Principal Fellows) down to higher diplomas for technical assistants, with corresponding requirements for research experience at each tier. All appointees will assist the research director in advancing railway electrification, automation, and related technological innovation.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This is a twelve-month postdoctoral position in industrial and systems engineering focused on assisting with research into self-healing reconstruction technology for snake robot formation. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree and have no more than five years of post-qualification experience. The role offers competitive remuneration, with applications closing on 26 June 2026.

title: Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This Research Assistant position in Industrial and Systems Engineering offers full-time or part-time opportunities to support a research project on hybrid energy field assisted ultra-precision diamond turning for a twelve-month appointment period. Candidates must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification and will assist the project leader with research activities. Full-time positions offer competitive remuneration, while part-time roles are compensated at HK$125 per hour. The distinctive focus is on advanced manufacturing technology combining energy fields with precision diamond turning processes.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering seeks to fill multiple research positions (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on a full-time or part-time basis for a 24-month appointment period. Appointees will support a research project focused on sonic metagrating barriers for broadband sound absorption and wave manipulation. Candidates must possess relevant qualifications ranging from an honours degree (for Research Assistant roles) to a doctoral degree with postdoctoral experience (for Research Fellow positions), along with demonstrated knowledge of acoustics, noise measurement, or vibration. The positions offer opportunities to contribute to an acoustics-focused research initiative within mechanical engineering.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering seeks Research Fellows, Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants (full-time or part-time, 24-month appointments) to support a project developing aural-comfortable headsets for people with hearing sensitivities and hearing loss. Candidates must possess expertise in acoustics, noise measurement, or vibration, with qualifications ranging from honours degree (for Research Assistants) to doctoral degree plus three years' postdoctoral experience (for Research Fellows). The appointees will work as a team assisting the project leader in the research and development phases of this specialized audio technology initiative.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering is recruiting for three tiered research positions (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on a full-time or part-time basis for a 24-month appointment. All appointees will support a project focused on designing and modeling a sway structural frame that integrates flow control, broadband noise reduction, and vibration suppression. Requirements vary by level, ranging from a doctoral degree plus three years of postdoctoral experience for Research Fellows to an honours degree for Research Assistants, with all candidates needing demonstrated knowledge in aerodynamics, acoustics, or vibration. Interested applicants should contact the project leader for further details.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: This posting covers three tiered positions within Mechanical Engineering, each offering a twelve-month appointment to support a research project on sustainable alkali and acid production from seawater for concrete recycling applications. The Postdoctoral Fellow role requires a doctoral degree with no more than five years post-qualification experience, while the Research Associate position requires a master's degree or strong honours degree plus three years of relevant research experience, and the Research Assistant role requires an honours degree. All applicants must demonstrate practical experience in electrochemical energy systems and will be offered competitive remuneration.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Part-time Project Technical Assistant
department: Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering
description: The Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering seeks a Postdoctoral Fellow (six-month initial appointment) and several part-time Project Technical Assistants (ten-month positions) to support research on 3D geometric feature computation for large aircraft. Appointees will conduct advanced research on point cloud techniques, develop algorithms for 3D perception tasks such as segmentation and localization, design validation experiments, and contribute to peer-reviewed publications and conference presentations while collaborating with multidisciplinary teams and industry partners. The Postdoctoral Fellow must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while Technical Assistants require an honours degree; all candidates need strong expertise in 3D perception, proficiency in Python and C++, demonstrated independent research ability, and excellent problem-solving skills. This role is distinctive for combining academic research with practical applications in aerospace engineering and requiring integration of multi-modal sensor data for complex

title: Research Fellow / Project Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate (Full-time/Part-time) / Research Assistant / Project Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: These positions span four roles across a 24-month appointment in metal forming and numerical modelling research. Responsibilities involve assisting the project leader with research on metal forming technologies and numerical modelling. Candidates require increasingly advanced qualifications depending on the level applied for, ranging from an honours degree for assistants to a doctoral degree plus three years of postdoctoral experience for research fellows. Strong backgrounds in materials processing technologies—particularly light alloys and energy materials—are essential across all positions, with preference given to those with expertise in computer programming, artificial intelligence, or machine learning.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The position involves supporting a research project on vision-based human digital twin modeling and scene understanding for human-robot collaboration, with three appointment levels available. The Postdoctoral Fellow position requires a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, while the Research Associate position requires a Master's degree or honors degree with at least three years of relevant experience. The Research Assistant position requires an honors degree or equivalent. All positions are for twelve-month appointments and involve assisting the project leader in research and related tasks.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering seeks a Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, or Research Assistant (full-time or part-time) for a twelve-month appointment to support research on ultrastrong-yet-ductile refractory high-entropy alloys with coherent nanoprecipitates. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree (for Postdoctoral Fellow, with ≤5 years post-qualification experience), master's/honours degree with ≥3 years research experience (for Research Associate), or honours degree (for Research Assistant). The role involves assisting the project leader with research activities on atomic structure, stability, and deformation mechanisms of advanced materials. Competitive remuneration is offered for full-time positions.

title: Project Fellow / Project Associate
department: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
description: This 12-month position offers administrative and technical support for a research project focused on developing intelligent tropical-storm-resilient systems for coastal cities. The Project Fellow role requires a doctoral degree with at least three years of postdoctoral experience, while the Project Associate role requires a Master's degree or strong honours degree with three years of relevant research or work experience. Candidates should have experience in climate-resilient infrastructure or large AI models, strong bilingual communication skills in English and Chinese, proficiency with standard office software, and the ability to work independently and collaboratively. The role is distinctive in its emphasis on supporting infrastructure resilience research in tropical contexts, combining technical expertise with administrative capability.

title: Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: A twelve-month Research Assistant position in Industrial and Systems Engineering is available on a full-time or part-time basis to support a project on magnetic field-assisted ultra-precision grinding of nickel-based superalloys. The role requires an honours degree or equivalent qualification and involves assisting the project leader with research activities. Full-time appointments offer competitive remuneration, while part-time positions pay HK$125 per hour. Applications close on 28 May 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate (Full-time/Part-time) / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering seeks six staff members across three levels (Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants) for a 12-18 month project on sustainable campus lighting and charging infrastructure with flexible multi-source power generation. Postdoctoral candidates must hold a doctorate with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, Research Associates require a master's degree or strong honours degree plus three years of relevant experience, and Research Assistants need an honours degree or equivalent. All appointees will support the project leader in research activities, with some positions available on full-time or part-time basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate (Full-time/Part-time) / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering is recruiting for multiple positions—four postdoctoral fellows, two research associates (full-time or part-time), and two research assistants (full-time or part-time)—on contracts of twelve to eighteen months. All appointees will support research on developing low-cost, vibration-durable in-wheel traction motors for next-generation electric vehicles. Postdoctoral fellows must hold a doctorate with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, research associates require a master's degree or strong honours degree plus three years of relevant experience, and research assistants need an honours degree or equivalent qualification.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This part-time research assistant position involves supporting a project leader on a twelve-month research initiative focused on hybrid 3D printing and nanoimprinting of ceramics for device casings, combining biomimetic design with sustainable manufacturing practices. Applicants must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification, with preference given to candidates with laser technology experience. The role is compensated at HK$125 per hour, with applications closing on 10 April 2026.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering seeks one Research Fellow and three Postdoctoral Fellows (each for twelve-month appointments) to assist with developing an intelligent ultra-precision air-bearing hydrostatic spindle for semiconductor wafer grinding. Research Fellow candidates must hold a doctoral degree plus at least three years of postdoctoral research experience, while Postdoctoral Fellow applicants require a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience. Positions offer competitive remuneration packages, with applications due by 27 April 2026.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering seeks to fill three positions—Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant—each for twelve months, to support a research project on optimizing optical design for enhanced visual experiences in virtual and augmented reality headsets. Postdoctoral Fellows must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-qualification experience, Research Associates require a Master's degree or strong honours degree with at least three years of relevant experience, and Research Assistants need an honours degree or equivalent. All appointees will assist the project leader in conducting the research. Interested applicants should contact the listed project supervisor for further details.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering is recruiting for four tiered research positions (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) on twelve-month contracts to support a project on the design and net-shape forming of micro-/meso-scaled surface functional structures. Requirements range from an honours degree for Research Assistants to a doctoral degree plus three years of postdoctoral experience for Research Fellows, with intermediate positions requiring master's degrees or doctorates with limited postdoctoral experience. All appointees will work under a project leader to advance research in precision manufacturing and materials processing. Interested candidates should contact the project supervisor for additional information.

title: Research Fellow / Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering seeks researchers at multiple levels (Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Fellow, Research Associate, and Research Assistant) to support a six-month project on adaptive 3D printing encompassing design, manufacturing, modeling, and optimization. Qualifications range from doctoral degrees with three years of postdoctoral experience for the most senior role to honours degree holders for the assistant position, with intermediate positions requiring doctorates or master's degrees paired with relevant research experience. All appointees will work under a project leader to advance research in advanced manufacturing technologies. Interested candidates should contact the project supervisor for additional details.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate / Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
description: The Department of Mechanical Engineering seeks postdoctoral fellows, research associates, or research assistants to support a research project developing hybrid neural-physical solvers that combine artificial intelligence with biomedical simulations. Candidates must have strong mathematical and coding skills with a solid mechanics background, with qualifications ranging from honours degrees to doctorates depending on the position level. All roles are full-time or part-time appointments lasting three to twelve months initially, with potential for renewal. Applicants should be self-motivated and may contact the project leader for additional information.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: The Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering is recruiting both a Postdoctoral Fellow and Research Assistant for three-month fixed-term positions to support a research project on multi-sensor-based smart sorting systems for waste recycling and sustainable development. Candidates for the Postdoctoral Fellow role must hold a doctoral degree with no more than five years of post-doctoral experience, while Research Assistant applicants need an honours degree or equivalent. Both positions require hands-on research experience in ultra-precision machining, precision metrology, smart defect detection, and smart testing and inspection, with competitive remuneration offered.

title: Research Assistant (Full-time/Part-time)
department: Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering
description: This position offers full-time or part-time research assistant roles within the Department of Industrial and Systems Engineering, with flexible appointment periods ranging from one to twelve months. Appointees will support a project leader on research involving magnetohydrodynamics-assisted ultra-precision diamond cutting for creating nano-patterned metallic surfaces. Applicants must hold an honours degree or equivalent qualification. The role offers competitive remuneration for full-time positions or HK$125 per hour for part-time work, with applications closing on 13 March 2026.

title: Research Assistant / Part-time Research Assistant
description: The School of Design seeks four research assistants (two full-time for three months, two part-time for six months) to support an Extended Reality and gamified urban furniture design project. Key responsibilities include developing Mixed Reality content in Unity for HoloLens, integrating gamification with Rhino3D tools, analyzing trans-disciplinary research, creating design frameworks, and mentoring students on platform use. Candidates must hold a master's degree in Architecture, Computer Science, or Engineering, with demonstrated expertise in XR software, gamified interactive content creation, and strong English communication skills, plus the ability to work independently and maintain project schedules.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Land Surveying and Geo-Informatics
description: This postdoctoral position involves assisting with a research project on remote sensing of urban climate, ecosystems, and environments using GeoAI approaches, with an appointment period of 12-36 months. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in a related field (such as remote sensing, GIS, computer science, AI, or environmental science) with no more than five years of post-doctoral experience, along with demonstrated experience in algorithm development, strong English proficiency, and at least two first-authored publications. The role strongly prefers candidates with expertise in quantitative methods, statistics, geospatial analysis, AI and computer vision, proficiency in Python and R, and experience with high-performance and cloud computing. Success requires the ability to work both independently and collaboratively within an interdisciplinary research team, supported by a strong publication record.

title: Part-time Helper
description: This part-time role in the Office of National Engagement involves assisting with content planning and writing aligned with the university's communication strategy, while providing operational support for new official media platforms. The ideal candidate should have prior experience in new media editing and operations, combined with strong skills in information research, editing, and organization, alongside proficiency in graphic design, photography, and video production. The position is offered on a three-month contract basis with potential for extension based on performance, and salary will reflect qualifications and experience. This is an equal opportunities role supporting the university's digital engagement and communication initiatives.

title: Assistant Human Resources Manager II
description: This Assistant Human Resources Manager II position involves managing core HR functions including recruitment, performance management, compensation, benefits, staff engagement, and HCMS administration, with particular emphasis on developing and coordinating employee onboarding and engagement initiatives. The role requires a bachelor's degree in Human Resources Management or related field, at least 5 years of relevant experience preferably in tertiary education, and fluency in English, Chinese, and Putonghua alongside proficiency with Excel and HCMS platforms. Candidates will provide executive support to HR-related university committees and contribute to the development and review of HR policies while implementing institutional HR projects and system enhancements. The position is distinctive in its focus on supporting a transforming institution emphasizing interdisciplinary research and human-centric technology innovation.

title: Assistant Human Resources Officer
description: The position is an Assistant Human Resources Officer responsible for providing administrative support to the HR Office and assisting with the coordination and administration of HR-related projects. Candidates should have relevant work experience in large organizations, preferably in higher education, alongside strong English and Chinese communication skills (including Putonghua). The role is offered on a fixed-term contract of one year with potential renewal, and compensation will be determined by qualifications and experience, supplemented by benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and mandatory provident fund contributions.

title: Assistant Administrative Officer (QA & E of TPg)
description: This role supports quality assurance and enhancement initiatives for taught postgraduate programmes, acting as a liaison between the graduate school and academic departments on admissions, programme approval, reviews, and policy compliance. Primary responsibilities include monitoring quality across postgraduate offerings, coordinating with programme teams, and managing logistics for related meetings and events. The successful candidate requires strong organizational and analytical abilities, bilingual fluency in English and Chinese (Putonghua), advanced MS Office proficiency, and capability to work autonomously and collaboratively. Prior higher education experience is preferred.

title: Administrative Manager (Research Postgraduate Team)
description: This Administrative Manager position supports the School of Graduate Studies in managing research postgraduate programmes across multiple functional areas, including academic policy, quality assurance, admissions, student records, and financial support. Key responsibilities involve day-to-day operational management, development of data systems and analytics platforms, and assistance with programme review and enhancement initiatives. The role requires 8 years of post-qualification experience preferably in higher education, substantial administrative expertise with demonstrated committee competency, and strong logical thinking and analytical capabilities.

title: Assistant Executive Officer (Research Postgraduate Team)
description: This administrative role supports research postgraduate programmes by managing student admissions, registration, records, assessments, and sponsorships within the School of Graduate Studies. The ideal candidate has 1-2 years of administrative experience (preferably in higher education), strong analytical and problem-solving skills, and fluency in English and Chinese (both Cantonese and Putonghua). The position is distinctive for its focus on logistical coordination of academic events and quality assurance activities that align with institutional expansion of postgraduate offerings. The role includes competitive compensation, professional development opportunities, and comprehensive benefits.

title: Assistant Library Officer (Learning Support)
description: This Assistant Library Officer position supports learning and instruction services by coordinating information literacy programs, library tours, and reference services in both online and in-person formats. Key responsibilities include managing the Course Readings service, maintaining library website content, creating promotional materials, and collecting usage statistics. The ideal candidate has 1-2 years of academic library experience, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Cantonese and Putonghua), strong PC skills with graphic design knowledge preferred, and availability for shift work including evenings and weekends. Knowledge of emerging technologies such as 3D modelling, printing, and laser cutting is distinctive and valued.

title: Lecturer
description: The position is a Lecturer in Philosophy responsible for teaching approximately ten courses annually at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels, supervising student projects, and contributing to departmental service and development. Candidates must hold a Master's degree or higher in Philosophy at the time of appointment. The role involves standard administrative duties as assigned and instruction conducted entirely in English. This position is distinguished by the department's strong reputation as one of Asia's leading philosophy programs, with faculty publishing regularly in prestigious international journals and supporting an active PhD program and postdoctoral fellowships.

title: Associate Director
description: This administrative leadership role oversees all operations within a graduate studies school, including research postgraduate admissions, quality assurance, policy development, and student support services. The successful candidate will require a bachelor's degree and 15 years of tertiary education experience, along with demonstrated management expertise, strong analytical and communication skills, and proven ability to coordinate effectively across committees and stakeholder groups. The position is distinctive in combining operational management with a strategic focus on enhancing postgraduate education quality and fostering international scholarly partnerships that support institutional growth objectives.

title: Senior Administrative Officer
department: Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries
description: This role supports a department head and programme director across administrative, curricular, and financial operations for an interdisciplinary creative arts department spanning digital media, film, and curatorial studies at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels. Key responsibilities include admissions management, budget planning, publicity coordination, and research support such as grant applications and compliance oversight. The position requires strong organizational capabilities and offers the distinctive opportunity to contribute to an innovative department that integrates traditional arts with digital technologies and regional economic impact.

title: Assistant Administrative Manager I (QA & E of TPg)
description: This position supports postgraduate programme administration across the institution, with a central focus on quality assurance and enhancement initiatives. The role encompasses admissions and policy management, programme reviews and validation, committee secretarial support, and institutional teaching and learning development. A successful candidate must bring five years of tertiary education administration experience, particularly in academic quality assurance, along with advanced proficiency in English and Putonghua Chinese. The distinctive aspect of this role is its university-wide remit for overseeing postgraduate programme quality across multiple faculties and schools within a research-intensive environment.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: This Research Assistant Professor position focuses on conducting high-impact research in contemporary areas such as digital discourse, digital humanities, or language and technology, with expectations for strong research productivity and interdisciplinary collaboration. The role involves supporting the department's research agenda in modern literature, discourse analysis, multimodality, and sociolinguistics while integrating with an innovative Master's programme that combines traditional English scholarship with digital tools and AI expertise. The position is distinctive for its emphasis on human-centric approaches to technology and offers opportunities to develop global academic partnerships while addressing modern challenges in language and communication.

title: Assistant Institutional Research Manager II
description: This position supports institutional research and data management by collecting, analyzing, and consolidating university data to inform strategic planning, budgeting, and enrollment decisions. Key responsibilities include producing analytical reports and dashboards for stakeholders, ensuring data accuracy and consistency across the institution, and collaborating with internal departments and IT teams to standardize data systems and processes. The role requires a Bachelor's degree in a quantitative or social science field, along with strong analytical and organizational skills to coordinate effectively across multiple university functions.

title: Senior Administrative Officer (TPg Programme Team)
description: This role is a Senior Administrative Officer supporting the School of Graduate Studies' postgraduate programmes, responsible for comprehensive administrative functions including quality assurance, admissions, registration, budgeting, assessments, and student records. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree with at least four years of tertiary education experience, preferably in taught postgraduate admissions and programme administration, alongside fluent English and Chinese (including Putonghua). Key competencies include strong communication, coordination, and problem-solving abilities, with capacity to work independently under pressure. The position offers competitive compensation, annual leave, medical and dental benefits, and mandatory provident fund contributions.

title: Administrative Officer (TPg Programme Team)
description: This Administrative Officer position supports the School of Graduate Studies in managing postgraduate programmes across quality assurance, admissions, registration, student records, assessment, and financial sponsorships. The role requires a bachelor's degree and at least four years of relevant experience in tertiary education, particularly in postgraduate admissions and programme administration, along with fluent English and Chinese (including Putonghua). Key competencies include strong interpersonal and communication skills, independent problem-solving ability, and the capacity to work under tight deadlines. The position is distinctive for its focus on strengthening central support for postgraduate education and monitoring programme quality across multiple faculties.

title: Programme Officer
description: This administrative role supports academic programmes in industrial data science, managing core functions including student admissions, registration, timetabling, examinations, and professional accreditation coordination. The post-holder will organize academic events such as seminars and conferences while liaising with internal and external stakeholders to deliver programmes effectively. Candidates require a degree in Business, Communication, Engineering, or Science, with strong bilingual English and Chinese proficiency and preferably familiarity with tertiary education systems in Hong Kong and mainland China. The position offers competitive compensation with comprehensive benefits, though occasional evening work and international travel may be required.

title: Assistant Programme Manager II / Senior Programme Officer
description: This position supports academic programmes in data science through administrative functions including student recruitment, registration, examinations, and advising, along with committee coordination and quality assurance. Candidates must have at least five years of tertiary education programme administration experience, exceptional academic writing abilities, and fluency in English, Chinese, Cantonese, and Putonghua. The role requires strong organizational skills to manage competing priorities independently while coordinating across departments and external partners, with occasional work required outside regular hours. The position offers competitive remuneration with comprehensive benefits including leave, healthcare coverage, and pension contributions.

title: Senior Programme Officer, Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office
department: Office of the Faculty of Business
description: This role oversees the administrative operations of taught postgraduate programmes within the Faculty of Business, managing admissions, scheduling, financial processes, and quality assurance activities. The position requires a Bachelor's degree with a minimum of four years of relevant experience, preferably in higher education, plus proficiency in English and Chinese. Key responsibilities include coordinating programme logistics, handling student registrations and visa applications, managing budgets and purchases, chairing committee duties, and leading programme team members. The distinctive aspect is the senior level requiring substantial prior experience and team leadership responsibilities to ensure seamless programme delivery.

title: Programmer (at the rank of Officer)
description: This programmer position requires developing and implementing web applications across desktop and mobile platforms using modern technologies like JavaScript frameworks (VueJS or NodeJS) to support organizational digital transformation. Candidates must hold a degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field with at least two years of web application development experience and strong analytical and English communication skills for effective team collaboration. The role offers competitive compensation commensurate with qualifications, supplemented by comprehensive benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Collection Development and Management Librarian (at the rank of Manager)
description: This managerial position leads collection development operations, overseeing resource acquisition, subscriptions, budgets, and vendor relations across all formats while supervising a dedicated team. The role demands a Master's in Library Science or equivalent and roughly 10 years of experience managing library collections, implementing policies, and fostering stakeholder relationships. A distinctive feature is the expectation of regular frontline duties, including reference desk coverage and shift work during evenings, weekends, and holidays, alongside strategic management responsibilities. The successful candidate will balance hands-on library services with oversight of the complete lifecycle of materials from selection through weeding.

title: Assistant Registry Services Officer
description: This Assistant Registry Services Officer position supports the Registry department's core functions across student admissions, registration, records management, timetabling, assessment, and programme administration. The role involves providing clerical and administrative support to Registry operations and committees, maintaining accurate records, and ensuring effective service delivery. Candidates should hold a post-secondary qualification with relevant administrative experience and demonstrate strong command of written and spoken English and Chinese, including Putonghua. The position offers competitive salary based on qualifications and experience, along with standard fringe benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, mandatory provident fund, and gratuity.

title: Programme Officer
description: This Programme Officer position supports a Doctor of Artificial Intelligence Studies program, requiring management of course scheduling, timetabling, class logistics, budgeting, and financial administration. The role involves serving as the primary student contact point while building relationships with faculty and external stakeholders, organizing outreach events, and preparing program reports. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree or higher (preferably with tertiary education experience), demonstrate strong project management and problem-solving abilities, and be fluent in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua). The position demands a self-motivated, proactive individual capable of multitasking and working independently in a fast-paced environment.

title: Lecturer / Assistant Lecturer (Two posts)
description: The Department of Economics is recruiting two Lecturer or Assistant Lecturer positions for economists in any specialization, with preference given to candidates experienced in teaching designated courses. Applicants must possess a Master's or PhD in economics from a reputable institution, demonstrate strong undergraduate teaching ability, and have at least five years of experience teaching undergraduate economics. The role primarily involves teaching undergraduate courses and contributing to departmental activities within a research-performing department that offers multiple degree levels and is undergoing digital transformation.

title: Senior Human Resources Officer
description: This role involves leading the implementation and optimization of an enterprise Human Capital Management system, including system migration, workflow design, and process improvement initiatives across the organization. The Senior Human Resources Officer will collaborate with IT teams and external vendors to integrate the system, translate HR requirements into actionable solutions, and manage user acceptance testing while engaging key stakeholders. Essential qualifications include a bachelor's degree in Human Resources Management, Information Systems, or related field, proven experience with HCM projects, strong knowledge of Hong Kong employment law and data privacy regulations, and fluency in English and Chinese with advanced Excel skills. The appointment level will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications and experience.

title: Programme Officer / Assistant Programme Officer
description: This position manages admissions, operations, and administrative functions for taught postgraduate psychology programmes, including application processing, student records management, and teaching logistics. Key responsibilities encompass programme promotion through digital and outreach channels, visa documentation handling, coordination with international partner institutions, and academic support services for postgraduate students. Candidates must have at least one year of work experience (ideally in higher education), fluency in English and Mandarin Chinese, and willingness to travel occasionally and work flexible hours. The role is distinctly focused on international student recruitment and cross-institutional partnerships, reflecting the department's strategic priority in building global academic connections.

title: Assistant Administrative Manager II
description: This role supports the Registry department in providing administrative services across academic governance, student services, and institutional operations. Key responsibilities include managing committee services, organizing events, overseeing assessment matters, and developing student administrative systems. The position requires a university degree with five years of relevant administrative experience, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua), strong interpersonal and communication skills, and demonstrated competency with IT systems and student administration platforms. The role is distinctive in its focus on supporting quality assurance and academic policy implementation across multiple faculties and schools, including newer interdisciplinary initiatives.

title: Research Assistant Professor (two posts)
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: The Department of Sociology and Social Policy is recruiting two Research Assistant Professors to conduct research and contribute to teaching in sociology, social policy, or computational social science. Candidates must hold a PhD obtained within the last five years in a related discipline and demonstrate strong research credentials through publications, a research portfolio, and teaching experience. The positions offer the opportunity to work within an internationally-oriented department that has a track record of securing competitive research grants and attracting postgraduate students globally, while contributing to the university's broader mission of developing human-centric technology and social policies.

title: Associate Professor of Teaching / Assistant Professor of Teaching
description: This teaching-focused position in the Department of Management requires delivering undergraduate and postgraduate courses while contributing to curriculum development and advancing the university's educational mission. The appointee must hold a doctorate in a relevant field and demonstrate excellence in teaching, with responsibilities including course instruction, programme development, and pursuit of knowledge transfer activities and teaching grants. The role emphasizes equipping students with practical knowledge and critical thinking skills for professional success in business, reflecting an institutional commitment to combining quality teaching with research-led education and human-centric innovation.

title: Accounting Officer
description: This accounting position encompasses complete financial cycle management, budget preparation, financial analysis, and statutory reporting for a research-focused academic institution with multiple campuses. The successful candidate will hold an accounting degree with professional certification (such as HKICPA), demonstrated experience in accounts management and financial reporting, and fluent bilingual English-Chinese abilities. The role demands strong analytical capability, meticulous attention to detail, and the capacity to work independently under pressure with competing deadlines while managing government-regulated cost recharging processes. The position is distinctive for its scope across diverse academic and administrative units within an institution undergoing digital transformation.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral fellowship invites early-career philosophers specializing in AI ethics to conduct independent research and publish in top-tier journals within a centre focused on catastrophic risk and technology governance. Candidates must have completed their PhD within the past three years and will work alongside an established cohort of postdoctoral researchers in a department known for analytic philosophy. The role is distinctive for its interdisciplinary positioning, bridging philosophy with applied policy concerns around AI and systemic risks. The position prioritizes scholarly productivity while offering engagement with an emerging intellectual landscape connecting ethics, artificial intelligence, and risk management.

title: Senior Officer / Officer (Short-term)
description: This is a short-term administrative position in the Office of Student Affairs supporting the Director and the broader office operations through secretarial, clerical, and data management tasks. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree with at least three years of relevant experience, ideally as a secretary or personal assistant to senior management, combined with strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua) and advanced computer skills. Key attributes include proactivity, organizational ability, attention to detail, and capacity to manage tight deadlines. The role is offered on a contract basis for up to five months, with salary and rank determined by qualifications and experience.

title: Assistant Clinic Officer (Full-time/Part-time), Chinese Medicine Clinic
description: This position is an Assistant Clinic Officer at a Chinese Medicine Clinic within the School of Graduate Studies, available on a full-time or part-time basis. The role encompasses front-desk operations including patient appointments, registration, and payment processing, alongside mentoring of student helpers and practicum students, with additional clerical duties as needed. Candidates should possess completion of Hong Kong secondary education with preferred professional training in Chinese Medicine, and must demonstrate fluency in Cantonese and Putonghua with basic English communication abilities. The clinic, established in 2020, serves as both a clinical service provider and an educational platform for student internships and health-related research within the university community.

title: Senior Information Officer (at the rank of Senior Officer)
description: This position manages and maintains a cloud-based ERP system, handling implementation, configuration, performance monitoring, and database maintenance across multiple platforms. Core responsibilities include overseeing data migration and ETL processes, developing APIs for system integration, and managing technical support for security, troubleshooting, and user accounts. The role requires an associate degree or higher in Computer Science or Information Technology, plus 2-3 years of experience in IT application systems and server management with strong cloud architecture knowledge. The position reports to the Team Leader of Database Services and involves collaboration with vendors and external consultants to maintain system reliability and compliance standards.

title: Analyst Programmer (at the rank of Senior Officer)
description: This senior analyst programmer role focuses on designing and developing scalable applications that modernize legacy systems through microservices architecture and cloud technologies like Microsoft Fabric to support academic and administrative operations. The position requires a Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science or a related field, along with demonstrated expertise in application development frameworks, containerization, CI/CD implementation, and secure coding practices. Key responsibilities include building data pipelines, integrating ERP and learning management systems, and establishing security and performance standards across the institution. The role is distinctive for its strategic emphasis on transforming outdated infrastructure into a modern, interconnected technology environment while maintaining institutional data integrity.

title: System Analyst (at the rank of Assistant Manager II)
description: This System Analyst role at assistant manager level leads full-cycle application development for admissions and records systems, encompassing requirements gathering, implementation, and ongoing maintenance while prioritizing the modernization of legacy applications using AI/ML and low-code technologies. Key responsibilities include translating business needs into technical solutions, managing cross-functional project delivery with internal and external teams, and ensuring quality through rigorous testing, secure coding practices, and CI/CD implementation. The position distinctively combines modernization initiatives with institutional compliance requirements and scalability considerations while exploring human-centric technology innovations. The role demands strong software engineering expertise and exceptional collaboration skills to operate effectively at the assistant manager level.

title: Assistant Administrative Officer
department: Pan Sutong Shanghai-Hong Kong Economic Policy Research Institute
description: This administrative support role serves a research institute focused on Shanghai-Hong Kong economic policy, requiring management of office operations, events coordination, and financial procedures. The ideal candidate possesses at least one year of work experience (preferably in higher education), fluent bilingual English and Chinese proficiency including both Cantonese and Putonghua, and the ability to multitask independently under deadline pressure. Primary responsibilities include stakeholder communication, organizing seminars and professional development events, preparing documentation, and overseeing procurement and reimbursement processes. The position offers a 12-month fixed-term contract with competitive remuneration, comprehensive benefits including leave, medical and dental coverage, and pension contributions.

title: Senior Student Affairs Officer
description: This role manages student amenities operations and coordinates key student activities including orientation, venue logistics, society registration, and leadership development programmes. The position requires a bachelor's degree and minimum five years of relevant administrative experience, preferably in higher education, with strong organisational skills and bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (Putonghua). A distinctive feature is the requirement for flexible hours including weekends to support campus activities and whole-person student development. The successful candidate will work independently within a team focused on enhancing the student experience and fostering student leadership.

title: Assistant Accounting Manager II (Systems) / Senior Accounting Officer (Systems)
description: This position leads the implementation of a new accounting system within an enterprise resource planning framework, encompassing user requirement compilation, workflow redesign, system configuration, and testing. The successful candidate requires an accounting degree with professional certification (such as HKICPA or ACCA), at least five years of supervisory experience, and two to three years specifically implementing integrated accounting systems. The role demands strong organizational and problem-solving skills, advanced MS Office competency, and deep knowledge of computerized accounting systems, with particular emphasis on bridging finance operations and IT collaboration to optimize system performance and organizational efficiency.

title: Associate Director of Communications and Public Affairs
department: Office of Communications and Public Affairs
description: This senior communications role involves developing and executing strategic initiatives to enhance institutional reputation through media relations, social media, publications, and events while managing team members at a Hong Kong-based research university. The position requires a minimum of 15 years of professional experience in corporate communications, public affairs, marketing, or event management, with demonstrated expertise in strategic communications and stakeholder relationship-building. Key responsibilities include promoting academic programmes, research initiatives, and technological innovations across multiple channels while supporting senior leadership in establishing the institution as a research and digital innovation leader. The successful candidate must combine strategic vision with hands-on management capability to shape external perceptions and drive stakeholder engagement.

title: Asso Professor of Teaching / Asst Professor of Teaching
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: This position is for an Associate or Assistant Professor of Teaching in the Department of Sociology and Social Policy, focusing primarily on teaching excellence rather than research productivity. The successful candidate will teach undergraduate and postgraduate courses in sociology, social policy, and related areas, and is expected to contribute to the department's internationally recognized teaching and research environment. Applicants must hold a PhD in Sociology, Social Policy, Social Work, Health Studies, or a related field, with demonstrated teaching experience, a strong teaching portfolio, and excellent English communication skills. The role is distinctive for its explicit emphasis on teaching quality within a department known for balancing both teaching and research excellence.

title: Senior Administrative Officer
description: This Senior Administrative Officer role oversees all departmental operations within Philosophy, supervising administrative staff and acting as the primary liaison between administrative teams and departmental leadership. Key responsibilities include coordinating undergraduate and postgraduate programme administration, ensuring operational consistency, and maintaining institutional knowledge during staff transitions. The position requires strong management and coordination capabilities, with a focus on supporting the Department's academic mission and strategic development. The role is distinctive in enabling the Department's international profile and expanding portfolio of academic events and external engagement activities.

title: Assistant Student Affairs Manager I
description: This position supports the integration of non-local students into the university community and Hong Kong society through culturally inclusive programming and cross-cultural initiatives. The role encompasses developing and managing student events, maintaining relationships with international student organizations, and coordinating programs such as cultural ambassador schemes and host family networks. The ideal candidate should be committed to fostering whole-person development and building an inclusive campus environment. The opportunity is distinctive for its focus on internationalization within an institution positioning itself as a leader in human-centric technology and social innovation.

title: Senior Project Officer
description: This Senior Project Officer position provides administrative and strategic support to the Dean of a data science school, encompassing event coordination, project management, budgeting, and grant proposal development. The role requires a Bachelor's degree and at least four years of tertiary education experience, combined with fluent bilingual English-Chinese capabilities in both written and verbal communication. Distinctive responsibilities include establishing research partnerships with Mainland institutions (requiring international travel), managing complex collaborative projects aligned with the university's strategic priorities in human-centric technology and social policy, and supporting organizational transformation. Strong proposal writing skills and demonstrated experience in building institutional partnerships are essential for success.

title: Assistant Programme Manager II / Senior Programme Officer
description: This position manages administrative operations for a newly established doctoral artificial intelligence programme, including course scheduling, budgeting, quality assurance, and stakeholder coordination. The role encompasses teaching coordination, experiential learning organization, financial compliance, and programme reporting for internal and external reviews. The successful candidate must balance operational efficiency with strategic programme development while supporting students, faculty, and external partners. The position is distinctive for its involvement in a transformative initiative centred on human-centric technology and innovation within a rapidly evolving field.

title: Senior Programme Officer
description: This role supports a newly established Artificial Intelligence division as a Senior Programme Officer, managing comprehensive administrative functions including proposal development, admissions, financial oversight, and quality assurance. The position encompasses operational management of teaching and learning, student services including registration and visa processing, scheduling, events, and budgeting. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree with at least four years of tertiary education experience and demonstrate fluency in English and Putonghua. The position is distinctive for supporting an innovative, interdisciplinary AI division within an institution undergoing digital transformation, emphasizing human-centric technology approaches.

title: HPC System Administrator
description: This HPC System Administrator position involves managing high-performance computing infrastructure and providing technical support to researchers through Linux administration, user account management, system monitoring, and daily operations. The role requires a bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field along with extensive hands-on experience administering large-scale Linux environments across multiple distributions, with preferred expertise in HPC job schedulers like Slurm and containerization technologies such as Docker and Singularity. The position is distinctive in combining traditional systems administration with specialized HPC capabilities to support the institution's strategic growth in data science and interdisciplinary research. Strong communication skills are essential for effectively serving a diverse research community.
