department: Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries
description: This position supports a creative arts department by managing specialized technical equipment for film, animation, motion capture, sound, and immersive media production. The ideal candidate demonstrates strong technical and computational skills with the ability to troubleshoot independently, learn new equipment rapidly, and perform preventative maintenance while supporting faculty and students. The role requires collaboration with IT services and facilities management to ensure continuous operation of technical resources. The position offers a distinctive opportunity to work at the intersection of creative education and advanced technology within a rapidly growing field.

title: Administrative Officer
description: This Administrative Officer role supports a Master's programme in International and Development Economics, with primary duties encompassing application processing, student enquiries, promotional coordination, and records management. The position requires a minimum of two years' work experience, ideally in higher education, along with fluent English and Mandarin Chinese communication skills and demonstrated teamwork abilities. Candidates with backgrounds in development economics, business, or prior course administration experience are preferred. The role involves occasional travel and flexible hours to meet programme demands.

title: Counsellor
description: The Counsellor position in the Office of Student Affairs involves providing individual and group psychological counselling to students, including those with special needs, and conducting psychological assessments to develop tailored support plans. Key responsibilities include designing and delivering wellness programmes, coordinating university-wide mental health initiatives, and supporting student integration across diverse backgrounds. The role requires supervising support staff and advising parents and university personnel on student wellbeing. This is a distinctive position emphasizing whole-person development within a transforming institution combining traditional strengths in arts and social sciences with emerging interdisciplinary research initiatives.

title: (Senior) Administrative Officer, Undergraduate Programmes Office
description: This administrative role supports an undergraduate programmes office within a business faculty, managing admissions, student advising, quality assurance, and accreditation processes. The successful candidate will need a degree and at least five years of administrative experience—ideally in higher education—with proficiency in admissions, event coordination, and advanced computer skills. Bilingual capability in English and Cantonese or Putonghua is required, along with strong analytical and collaborative abilities. The position offers competitive compensation with standard benefits including leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Administrative Officer
description: This position provides comprehensive administrative support to a Philosophy Department, with particular focus on a newly launched undergraduate programme and research operations. Key responsibilities include assisting senior leadership with programme planning and coordination, organizing departmental events, managing budgets and finances, handling secretarial duties for leadership and committees, and coordinating stakeholder engagement. The role is distinguished by its placement in a research-intensive philosophy department emphasizing interdisciplinary work and human-centered innovation, requiring someone capable of supporting both established operations and new programme development.

title: Assistant Executive Officer (Office Admin & General Service Team)
description: This administrative role supports a graduate studies office by managing daily operations, student services, and event coordination while handling budgeting, human resources, and counter services for postgraduate students and stakeholders. The ideal candidate brings at least three years of administrative experience (preferably in higher education), advanced MS Office skills, and bilingual fluency in English and Chinese including both Cantonese and Putonghua. What distinguishes this position is its integration of diverse administrative functions within a research-focused graduate school context, with a strategic focus on improving postgraduate education quality. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities are essential for succeeding in this multifaceted role.

title: Senior Information Security Analyst
description: This Senior Information Security Analyst position oversees security operations by conducting security testing, threat modeling, and incident investigations while managing advanced tools such as SAST, DAST, and SIEM platforms. The role uniquely combines proactive security functions—including security architecture guidance and a Security Champions Program—with reactive incident response and threat intelligence work. Core responsibilities also include ensuring data protection compliance and delivering cybersecurity awareness training across the organization. The position requires a Bachelor's degree and current expertise in information security practices and emerging threat landscapes.

title: Information Manager (Virtualisation Services)
description: This role manages virtualization and storage infrastructure including servers, hyperconverged systems, SAN storage, and IoT platforms, with responsibility for capacity planning, lifecycle management, backup policies, and network support. Candidates require a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or IT, a minimum of 8 years' IT experience (ideally including 3 years in management), and demonstrated expertise in enterprise virtualization technologies. The position is distinctive for its focus on emerging technologies such as container orchestration and large-scale IoT deployment, positioning it at the intersection of operational infrastructure and innovation. The role demands shift work and irregular hours due to the mission-critical nature of the infrastructure managed.

title: Assistant Accounting Manager I
description: This Assistant Accounting Manager I position in the Finance Office requires supervising an accounting team responsible for monthly payroll cycles, compiling staff cost budgets across departments, and improving workflow efficiency. The ideal candidate holds an accounting degree and professional certification (HKICPA/ACCA), with at least 10 years of accounting experience including 3+ years in management, strong Excel and accounting software skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure. Prior experience in higher education or the non-profit sector is preferred. The role offers competitive salary and benefits including annual leave, medical/dental coverage, and retirement contributions, with review continuing until the position is filled.

title: Senior Administrative Manager (Research Postgraduate Team)
description: This Senior Administrative Manager position oversees all administrative operations for research postgraduate programmes, including admissions, course registration, student records, and financial support. The role requires at least 10 years of post-qualification experience, preferably in tertiary education, combined with strong analytical and interpersonal skills and fluency in English and Putonghua Chinese. Key responsibilities extend beyond day-to-day operations to include supporting academic development, quality assurance initiatives, and contributing to policy formulation and programme reviews. The position is distinctive for its specialisation in research postgraduate administration within an institution focused on interdisciplinary research and international academic collaboration.

title: Visiting English Tutor
department: Centre for English and Additional Languages
description: This visiting position involves delivering English language support through tutorials, workshops, and self-access materials to undergraduate and postgraduate students at a dedicated language centre. Key responsibilities include organizing academic and social events that develop English skills while fostering cultural exchange between international tutors and local students. Candidates must hold a recent Bachelor's or Master's degree from designated partner institutions and demonstrate advanced English proficiency (IELTS 8.0 or above). The role is distinctive for its emphasis on building interpersonal relationships and cultural interaction alongside formal language instruction, actively seeking applicants committed to meaningful engagement with the student community.

title: Associate Director (Application Development)
description: This role leads strategic application development for enterprise-level systems supporting academic, administrative, and research operations, reporting directly to the CIO & University Librarian. The Associate Director manages a team of over 16 developers, overseeing the complete software development lifecycle and directing a major migration to a new Enterprise Resource Planning system. The position requires a leader with strong technical expertise, proven management capabilities, and the ability to champion secure, scalable, user-focused solutions across departments. Success demands both technical depth and cross-functional collaboration to address complex institutional technology needs.

title: Assistant Administrative Officer (TPg Programme Team)
description: This position supports the School of Graduate Studies by providing comprehensive administrative assistance to taught postgraduate programmes, including student admission, data management, teaching coordination, examinations, and quality assurance. The role encompasses counter services, office administration, and student activity support. Candidates must demonstrate strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua) and competency with Microsoft Office and web applications. The appointment is contract-based for up to 11 months, with salary commensurate with qualifications and experience, and includes standard benefits such as annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and mandatory provident fund contributions.

title: Senior Research Officer/Research Officer
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: This position involves conducting data analysis and research support for a longitudinal study examining discrimination and health-risk behavior within the Department of Sociology and Social Policy. The successful candidate must hold a master's degree in social or behavioral sciences and demonstrate strong proficiency in statistical programming (Stata, Mplus, or R) alongside solid quantitative research methodology skills. Preference will be given to candidates with expertise in health or aging research and experience handling large-scale or panel datasets. The role is offered on a 12-month fixed-term contract with remuneration based on qualifications and experience, including standard benefits such as leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Assistant Research Officer
description: This position is an Assistant Research Officer within the Department of Accountancy, requiring knowledge of textual analysis using Python or similar programming languages. The role is contract-based initially for up to six months with potential renewal, and the salary will be determined by qualifications and experience. The appointment includes standard benefits such as annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and mandatory provident fund contributions. The position supports the university's commitment to interdisciplinary research and innovation, particularly within its broader initiative to establish itself as a hub for human-centric technology and social policy development.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Institute of Insurance and Risk Management
description: This Research Assistant Professor position at a newly established insurance and risk management institute requires conducting scholarly research, securing external funding, and teaching at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels while contributing to institutional service. Applicants must hold a PhD in Risk and Insurance or related field earned within the past five years and demonstrate strong research credentials. The role is distinctive for its emphasis on practice-oriented research that connects academic work directly to industry applications, including organizing forums, conferences, and applied surveys designed to support the region's insurance sector development.

title: Assistant Professor of Teaching
department: Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries
description: This teaching-focused assistant professor position seeks an expert in digital animation, VR/AR, interactive art, or cinematography to teach undergraduate and graduate courses and supervise capstone projects within an interdisciplinary creative department. The role emphasizes bridging emerging digital technologies with creative practice through a human-centric approach, while contributing to departmental growth and supporting the region's creative industries sector. Candidates must demonstrate teaching excellence and deep expertise in contemporary digital technologies and creative methodologies applicable across film, animation, digital media, and curatorial studies. The position is distinctive for its explicit focus on innovation and interdisciplinary collaboration rather than research productivity.

title: Assistant Library Officer (Cataloguing and Preservation)
description: This role focuses on cataloguing and preserving library materials using international standards and the Alma Library System, while maintaining bibliographic records and compiling relevant statistics, with additional counter service duties including evening and weekend shifts. Candidates should possess cataloguing experience in an academic library, proficiency in Resource Description & Access and MARC21 standards, and fluency in both English and Chinese. Experience with integrated library systems, particularly Alma/Primo, is preferred but not essential. The position offers competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement provisions, with openness to candidates at various career stages.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow - Philosophy of Contemporary & Future Science
description: This postdoctoral position offers a one to two-year appointment for researchers with expertise in philosophy of science, formal epistemology, AI, or philosophy of specific sciences. The fellow will conduct research within a five-year institutional fellowship focused on philosophy of contemporary and future science, working in an interdisciplinary environment alongside both philosophers and scientists. The role is distinctive for its embeddedness in a broader institutional initiative addressing human-centric technology and social policy, positioning the fellow to engage with urgent philosophical questions at the intersection of science and emerging technologies. The position offers membership in an active postdoctoral community within a philosophy department recognized for research excellence and high-impact publications.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer
description: The Department of Translation is recruiting a Senior Lecturer or Lecturer to deliver courses spanning computer-aided translation, business translation, interpreting, translation technology, and cultural studies, with emphasis on bilingual Chinese-English instruction that merges professional practice with academic scholarship. Applicants must possess a master's degree in Translation Studies or related discipline and demonstrate fluency in Chinese and English, with Cantonese and Putonghua proficiency preferred; Senior Lecturer candidates require a minimum of eight years' university teaching experience. The role is distinctive for its integration of translation as both a practical profession and scholarly field, contributing to the institution's development as a center for human-centric technology and interdisciplinary research. The position offers competitive compensation alongside comprehensive benefits including medical, dental, and relocation support.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Office of the Faculty of Business
description: This position is a Research Assistant Professor within the Faculty of Business, requiring the holder to conduct independent and collaborative research while teaching undergraduate and postgraduate courses. The ideal candidate will hold a PhD in management science, operations research, or a related field earned within the past five years, with demonstrated research excellence and quality publications. The role involves working across the Office of the Faculty of Business and the Hong Kong Institute of Business Studies, with potential administrative responsibilities. This is a distinctive opportunity within an institution emphasizing human-centric technology, social policies, and interdisciplinary collaboration through its advanced research institute.

title: Part-time Lecturer
department: Centre for English and Additional Languages
description: This part-time position seeks an Academic English Language Lecturer to teach undergraduate courses focused on developing English proficiency and critical thinking skills within a center dedicated to language support. Candidates must have university-level teaching experience, preferably in Hong Kong, and hold at least a master's degree in English Language and Literature or a related field, with a PhD preferred. The role emphasizes expertise in foundational academic English instruction and advanced skills such as academic writing, critical argumentation, seminar discussion, and reflective practices. Compensation is competitive and based on qualifications and experience, with appointments made on a contract basis.

title: Sr. Administrative Officer / Administrative Officer
department: LEO Dr David P. Chan Institute of Data Science
description: This administrative role provides comprehensive support to a data science institute director, encompassing event management, stakeholder relationship building, and funding development alongside operational functions such as budgeting, procurement, and financial oversight. The position requires a Bachelor's degree with a minimum of 5 years' experience in education (or less for the Officer-level position), bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese including Putonghua, and technical skills in Microsoft PowerPoint, Adobe Creative Suite, and Chinese word processing. Success in the role demands strong teamwork, initiative, analytical ability, and the capacity to manage multiple concurrent responsibilities under tight deadlines while maintaining regulatory compliance.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: This Research Assistant Professor position requires a recently earned PhD in Sociology, Social Policy, or computational social science, with demonstrated strength in both research and teaching. The role emphasizes research productivity and publication output, requiring candidates to submit comprehensive evidence of their research portfolio, publications, and teaching effectiveness. The department seeks scholars who can contribute to its internationally recognized research profile, secure competitive grants, and support interdisciplinary, human-centric research initiatives while teaching at undergraduate and postgraduate levels. Strong communication skills and commitment to advancing the department's research reputation are essential.

title: Assistant Administrative Officer
description: This Assistant Administrative Officer position in the Department of Economics provides comprehensive administrative support for undergraduate programs, including admissions, academic advising, assessments, and student services. Key responsibilities include coordinating student and teaching activities, maintaining departmental records, and managing admission interviews, visa applications, and registration processes. The role requires proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and webpage technology. The position is offered on a 12-month contract basis with potential for reappointment based on performance, with compensation and benefits determined by qualifications and experience.

title: Senior Lecturer / Lecturer
department: Department of Sociology and Social Policy
description: This position is a teaching and research role in sociology and social policy at a Hong Kong university, requiring the successful candidate to deliver undergraduate courses in health and social care, fundraising, and program management. Applicants must possess a PhD in Sociology, Social Policy, Social Work, Health Studies, or a closely related discipline, along with proven teaching experience and strong English proficiency. The department is research-active with strong international publication credentials and grant-winning capacity, expecting new faculty to contribute to both undergraduate and postgraduate education. The role is distinctive for its alignment with the institution's mission to develop human-centric technology and social policy in response to digital transformation.

title: Associate Director/Senior Manager/Manager
description: This role involves developing and executing the university's internationalisation strategy, including building and maintaining international partnerships and overseeing student mobility programmes. The successful candidate will also be responsible for reviewing and enhancing international student recruitment initiatives. Strong interpersonal, communication, and leadership skills are essential, alongside experience in higher education administration. The position offers flexibility in rank and salary based on qualifications, with competitive benefits including leave, medical coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Director of Research and Knowledge Transfer
department: Office of Research and Knowledge Transfer
description: This senior leadership position directs an institution's research strategy and knowledge transfer operations, managing funding applications, research partnerships, and commercialization of outputs to maximize institutional and societal impact. The role demands at least 15 years of higher education experience with demonstrated expertise in research strategy, grant management, knowledge transfer, and corporate-level reporting, alongside fluency in English, Chinese, and Putonghua. The position distinctively focuses on establishing the institution as a global leader in human-centric technology and social policy innovation through international collaboration and regional research development. The Director serves as a key strategic advisor to senior management on research policy while driving technology commercialization and policy innovation initiatives.

title: Administrative Officer /Assistant Administrative Officer (TPg Admission)
description: This role supports the School of Graduate Studies by managing administrative operations for taught postgraduate programmes, with primary responsibilities including student admissions, registration, document verification, and coordination of outreach activities to mainland China. The position also handles financial tasks such as purchases, reimbursements, and budgeting, while the Administrative Officer level additionally supervises junior staff. Candidates require at least two years of experience in tertiary education administration, preferably in postgraduate admissions and programme promotion, alongside fluent English and Chinese (including Putonghua). The role demands strong initiative, the ability to work under pressure, and collaborative teamwork with a high sense of responsibility.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: The position is a Research Assistant Professor at the Hong Kong Catastrophic Risk Centre, a philosophy-focused research unit seeking someone with expertise in philosophy of AI and/or ethics of risk. The successful candidate must hold a PhD obtained within the past five years and demonstrate a publication record in leading philosophy journals. The role offers the opportunity to conduct high-impact research within a department recognized as one of Asia's strongest in analytic philosophy, while contributing to the centre's mission of addressing catastrophic risks through philosophical inquiry.

title: Assistant Administrative Manager II/Sr. Administrative Officer
description: This role provides administrative leadership for a newly established artificial intelligence research division, overseeing external communications, branding, and publicity through multiple channels including website management. The position requires a bachelor's degree with at least 5 years of higher education experience, bilingual fluency in English and Chinese, and demonstrated organizational and communication capabilities. Primary responsibilities encompass event coordination, budget management, reporting, and strategic dissemination of research outcomes to varied stakeholders. The distinctive aspect lies in the opportunity to build foundational administrative structures for an emerging division positioned within a broader institutional commitment to human-centered technology and social policy research.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: The position is a tenure-track role in the Department of English specializing in Modern Literature, requiring the successful candidate to teach undergraduate and postgraduate courses while supervising student projects, conduct high-impact research and secure competitive grants, and contribute to departmental administration and curriculum development. The appointee should demonstrate strong teaching effectiveness and substantial potential for quality research output in the near term. Candidates with experience in digital humanities, modern literature, or world literatures are particularly valued. The institution emphasizes excellence in both teaching and research, with competitive compensation and benefits packages commensurate with qualifications and experience.

title: Manager (Technology Transfer and Business Development)
department: Office of Research and Knowledge Transfer
description: This position manages the commercialization of university research by overseeing intellectual property portfolios, identifying patentable innovations, and marketing inventions to industry partners across Hong Kong, mainland China, and international markets. Key responsibilities include negotiating licensing agreements, organizing technology marketing events, maintaining transfer databases and websites, and producing marketing materials and reports. The role requires a bachelor's degree in science or engineering with demonstrated experience in technology transfer, business development, and event management, plus fluency in English and Chinese. Compensation and appointment level are flexible based on qualifications, with a competitive benefits package including salary, leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Professor of Teaching / Associate Professor of Teaching / Assistant Professor of Teaching
description: This teaching-focused position in the Marketing and International Business Department is available at Assistant, Associate, or Professor rank for candidates with a doctoral degree in Marketing or a related field. Primary responsibilities encompass teaching core and elective courses, developing innovative curricula and pedagogical approaches, coordinating programmes, mentoring students, and engaging in knowledge transfer activities. The role emphasises educational impact and holistic student development, with encouragement to pursue teaching grants and drive curriculum innovation. Appointment rank and salary are commensurate with qualifications and experience, and the position offers comprehensive benefits including leave, medical and dental coverage, and relocation support.

title: Professor
department: Department of Marketing and International Business
description: This Professor position requires a PhD in Marketing or related field with an established track record of publishing in top-tier journals and securing competitive research grants. The role combines active research leadership with teaching responsibilities across undergraduate and postgraduate levels, alongside contributions to departmental strategy and curriculum development. Prior administrative experience and success in pursuing RGC and international funding are valued attributes. The appointment is structured as an initial three-year contract with potential for longer-term substantiation.

title: Senior Research Engineer
description: This Senior Research Engineer position develops humanitarian technologies through an academic UN Hub, applying AI, data analytics, and IoT to address development challenges in the Global South and support sustainable development goals. The role requires strong expertise in AI and data analytics combined with a genuine commitment to humanitarian impact, and involves working with interdisciplinary teams to convert research concepts into practical solutions. A distinctive feature is the integration of teaching responsibilities, including delivering lectures for a Master's programme in global humanitarian innovation, which merges research output with academic mentorship. The position prioritizes human-centric, impact-oriented technology development across diverse applications ranging from medical devices to environmental solutions.

title: Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: This position seeks a PhD-qualified finance scholar to teach and conduct research within a liberal arts framework, combining undergraduate and postgraduate instruction with scholarly publication in top-tier journals. Candidates must demonstrate strong research potential, secure external funding from competitive sources, and contribute to departmental service. Preferred candidates will have an established publication record, professional certifications like CFA, prior teaching or administrative experience, and connections to Hong Kong's financial sector, alongside excellent communication abilities.

title: Professor / Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: The Department of History seeks a Professor, Associate Professor, or Assistant Professor specializing in Asia in global contexts, with a PhD in History or related field and a strong record of peer-reviewed publications and teaching excellence. The successful candidate must contribute to the department's strategic priorities in global Asia, public history, and/or digital history, with digital humanities experience considered advantageous. Key responsibilities include conducting high-impact research, securing competitive grant funding, and developing innovative courses. The position offers competitive remuneration commensurate with qualifications, along with comprehensive benefits including leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement contributions.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: This Research Assistant Professor position seeks an early-career historian specializing in global history, particularly from underrepresented regions such as South Asia, Southeast Asia, Europe, and the Americas, or possessing expertise in digital historical methods. Applicants must hold a PhD obtained within the past five years, demonstrate a strong track record of publications and grant acquisition, and teach two to three courses annually while contributing to institutional service. The role prioritizes scholarly productivity through international publications and competitive research funding, with preference given to candidates combining digital research capabilities with innovative teaching experience.

title: Assistant Professor
description: The Department of History seeks an Assistant Professor specializing in United States history with a transnational or global perspective, requiring a PhD in History or a related field. The successful candidate must demonstrate excellence in research and teaching, with ability to teach U.S. history courses and contribute to departmental priorities including global Asia, public history, and digital history; experience in digital humanities is advantageous. The position is tenure-track with an initial three-year contract potentially leading to longer-term appointment and substantiation, offering competitive remuneration and comprehensive benefits. The appointee will be expected to actively seek external funding through RGC grants and international competitive schemes.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries
description: The position is a fixed-term Research Assistant Professor role in the Department of Digital Arts and Creative Industries focused on teaching and advancing the MA in Creative and Media Industries programme. The appointee will teach graduate-level courses, supervise capstone projects, and contribute to departmental development and service. The ideal candidate holds a PhD in film and media studies with a strong publication record, research awards, and success in securing competitive grants.

title: Senior Administrative Officer / Administrative Officer
description: This position supports the Division of Artificial Intelligence by providing comprehensive administrative services, including financial management, procurement, reimbursements, and budgeting. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree with relevant tertiary education sector experience (minimum four years for the Senior level) and demonstrates fluent written and verbal English and Chinese proficiency, alongside strong analytical and interpersonal skills. The role is distinctive in supporting a newly established division within a university undergoing transformation into a hub for human-centric technology research and innovation. Competitive remuneration is offered alongside standard fringe benefits including leave, healthcare coverage, and pension contributions.

title: Research Assistant Professor
description: This Research Assistant Professor position seeks a philosopher with expertise in Set Theory or strong teaching credentials in logic-related courses such as Critical Thinking, Philosophy of Artificial Intelligence, or Deductive Logic. The role requires a PhD in Philosophy or a related field and involves teaching specialized coursework while potentially freeing departmental colleagues to focus on Set Theory instruction. The position offers involvement with newly launched philosophy programs, including a Bachelor of Arts in Philosophy and AI and a Master of Arts in Artificial Intelligence and the Future, within a department recognized for excellence in analytic philosophy across aesthetics, ethics, Chinese philosophy, and philosophy of mind.

title: Research Officer
department: The Advanced Institute for Global Chinese Studies
description: This Research Officer position supports the director of an institute focused on global Chinese studies by coordinating research projects across humanities disciplines, with emphasis on Chinese literature and culture. Key responsibilities include organizing seminars and international conferences, producing video content on Chinese topics, editing publications, and managing external communications with contributors and media partners. Candidates should demonstrate experience in project design and operational management, and the role offers competitive salary and comprehensive benefits including leave, medical coverage, and retirement contributions. The position is based at a Hong Kong institution committed to interdisciplinary research and global scholarly collaboration.

title: Chair Professor
description: This senior academic leadership position in Accountancy requires a PhD holder with extensive teaching experience and a strong international research profile demonstrated through high-impact publications and secured grants. The role combines research and teaching responsibilities with departmental and faculty management duties, emphasizing alignment with the institution's strategic priorities in AI, data science, and STEM-related accounting research. Candidates with prior administrative experience at departmental or faculty level are preferred. The appointee must maintain excellence across teaching, research, and service while supporting faculty development and contributing to institutional initiatives.

title: Senior Programme Officer / Programme Officer
description: This position supports the Doctor of Artificial Intelligence Studies program, requiring management of course scheduling, student logistics, budgeting, and stakeholder relationships across academic and administrative functions. The role demands a bachelor's degree and at least five years of experience in program management, administrative support, or event coordination in tertiary or executive education settings. Key responsibilities include organizing outreach events, coordinating teaching activities, handling financial tasks, and serving as the primary student contact. The ideal candidate is self-motivated, proactive, with strong project management capabilities and experience managing high-level programs or events.

title: Senior Programme Officer / Programme Officer
description: This position involves managing administrative and promotional activities for academic programs within a data science division, including developing marketing materials, updating digital platforms, organizing events, and creating content for social media campaigns to increase engagement and visibility. Candidates should hold a bachelor's degree in marketing, public relations, event management, business administration, or a related field, with at least four years of relevant experience for the senior level role. Essential skills include proficiency with Microsoft Office and Adobe Creative Suite, alongside strong organizational abilities and multitasking capabilities. Preference is given to applicants with knowledge of the higher education sector in Hong Kong and mainland China.

title: Associate Professor of Teaching / Assistant Professor of Teaching / Senior Lecturer
description: The position is for a teaching-focused Associate/Assistant Professor or Senior Lecturer within the School of Graduate Studies, specializing in internationalisation and quality management in higher education. The role encompasses teaching and learning activities, academic program coordination and management, organization of research events and knowledge transfer activities, and related administrative duties. Candidates must hold a PhD in a related discipline and demonstrate at least five years of relevant teaching experience with a track record in academic program development. This appointment supports the university's strategic expansion of postgraduate education and its emphasis on interdisciplinary research, particularly in policy studies, health studies, and higher education.

title: Associate Professor/Assistant Professor
description: The position is an Associate/Assistant Professor role in Artificial Intelligence focused on conducting high-quality research in areas such as natural language processing, large language models, and computer vision, while securing external research funding. Responsibilities include teaching undergraduate and postgraduate courses in data science and AI, managing admissions and programme operations, and performing administrative duties. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in a relevant field with a strong publication and citation record, good command of both English and Chinese, and ideally possess experience in grant applications and academic programme administration; Associate Professor applicants should have at least six years of tenure-track academic experience.

title: Senior Administrative Officer / Administrative Officer
department: Institute of Insurance and Risk Management
description: This role provides administrative support to the Institute of Insurance and Risk Management and the Department of Operations and Risk Management, handling various departmental activities and operations. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree with at least three years of administrative experience, ideally in higher education, alongside strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (Mandarin) and demonstrated computer literacy, including website maintenance skills. The position requires the ability to work both collaboratively within teams and independently, with salary and rank determined competitively based on qualifications and experience. Additional benefits include annual leave, medical and dental coverage, mandatory provident fund contributions, and gratuity.

title: Part-time Clerk
description: This part-time clerk position at a self-financing further education institute in central Hong Kong offers flexible scheduling between 18-30 hours weekly, including potential weekend and evening work. The role requires secondary school completion as a minimum, with preference for associate degree or higher qualifications, alongside proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua) and competence in Microsoft Office and Chinese word processing. The position is distinctive for its flexible work arrangements and placement within an educational environment delivering tertiary and continuing education programmes. Salary will be based on qualifications and experience, with immediate availability preferred.

title: Senior Programme Officer/Programme Officer,Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office
description: This role manages taught postgraduate programmes in the Faculty of Business, overseeing admissions, registration, scheduling, financial operations, quality assurance, and committee support. Candidates require a Bachelor's degree plus two to four years of tertiary education experience (depending on seniority level), combined with fluent English and Chinese language skills. Key responsibilities include student services coordination from admission to completion, examination and event management, and institutional reporting. The position offers leadership opportunities and the chance to contribute to the institution's strategic development as an interdisciplinary research and innovation centre.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: JC STEM Early Career Research Fellowship for Translation and Application (24/25)
description: This postdoctoral fellowship supports early-career STEM researchers for three years of full-time research at the institution, with potential for an additional year of industry-based translational research attachment. The position offers comprehensive financial support including an annual stipend of up to $530,000 plus a 15% completion gratuity, alongside professional development funding of up to $30,600 annually. The fellowship aims to help researchers advance their careers, expand professional networks, and generate social impact through translational research and innovation. Candidates from around the world are eligible, and the position is particularly suited for researchers interested in bridging academic research with practical, real-world applications in industry settings.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: LU Presidential Postdoctoral Research Fellowship Scheme
description: This postdoctoral fellowship scheme welcomes early-career researchers globally who have received their PhD within the past three years and demonstrated strong research potential and publication records. Fellows will work collaboratively within a supportive research environment across the institution's diverse faculties and interdisciplinary institutes, with access to resources, expertise, and mentorship to advance their research goals. The position offers a three-year appointment structure, beginning with a one-year contract with potential extension for two additional years based on satisfactory performance. Appointees are required to pursue external funding through specified local and international grant schemes, positioning them for independent research careers.

title: Chair Professor / Professor / Associate Professor / Assistant Professor
description: This is a recruitment for tenure-track academic positions across multiple ranks (from Assistant Professor to Chair Professor) in any discipline, with strategic priority given to interdisciplinary scholars connecting STEM with humanities, business, and social sciences, particularly those working on sustainable energy, carbon neutrality, environmental sustainability, and smart infrastructure. Candidates must hold a PhD, with experience and research standing appropriate to their target rank. The university offers competitive compensation and funding packages, including eligibility for government-sponsored STEM professorships and an internal early-career scholars scheme providing enhanced salary and start-up grants.

title: Professor and Director
department: Pan Sutong SH-HK Economic Policy Research Institute
description: This position combines a full professorship in Economics with directorship of an economic policy research institute serving mainland China and Hong Kong. The role demands both strategic leadership of research operations and extensive stakeholder engagement with academic and industry partners, alongside building international collaborative networks. Candidates must demonstrate exceptional scholarship in economic development policy relevant to both regions, alongside proven excellence in research, teaching, and knowledge exchange. The distinctive nature of this role lies in its dual emphasis on rigorous academic work and direct policy influence, positioning the appointee as a bridge between two major economies.

title: Administrative Officer, Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office
description: This administrative position supports postgraduate taught programmes within a business faculty, focusing on stakeholder engagement, digital marketing, and campaign coordination. The successful candidate requires a Bachelor's degree with at least two years of relevant experience, ideally in higher education, alongside bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (Putonghua). Key competencies include strong communication and teamwork abilities, analytical skills, and the capacity to work independently under tight deadlines across diverse responsibilities. The role offers competitive remuneration with comprehensive benefits including annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and retirement provisions.

title: Student Advising Officer
department: Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine (20000)
description: This two-year fixed-term position in the Student Wellness Team requires a Master's degree in a mental health discipline (Counselling, Social Work, or Counselling Psychology) with at least three years of post-qualification experience, preferably in tertiary education or mental health agencies, and demonstrated fluency in English and Cantonese. The role centers on coordinating and delivering mental health programmes, wellness initiatives, trainings, and events while providing individual and group counselling, crisis intervention, and walk-in services under supervision. Key distinguishing factors include working specifically with high-achieving student populations and designing priority-based interventions to promote student wellbeing. The position offers competitive salary, annual leave, medical benefits, and retirement contributions of up to 10% of basic salary, with potential renewal subject to performance and funding.

title: Assistant Manager (at the rank of Executive Officer)
description: This is a two-year fixed-term Executive Officer position in the Technology Transfer Office responsible for administering and coordinating an innovation funding scheme (RAISe+), including project monitoring, compliance verification, and event organization to facilitate collaboration between academia, industry, and government. The role requires a bachelor's degree and at least three years of experience in project management, innovation, or technology transfer, with strong organizational skills, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, and proficiency in Microsoft Office and project management systems. Preferred qualifications include experience in tertiary education or research environments and knowledge of company secretarial practices, intellectual property management, or innovation funding schemes. The position offers competitive salary, annual leave, medical benefits, and a University retirement contribution of up to 10% of basic salary, with the possibility of contract renewal subject to performance and funding availability.

title: Executive Officer / Senior Executive Assistant (at the rank of Clerk I)
department: Department of Psychiatry, School of Clinical Medicine (21500)
description: This is a one-year temporary or two-year fixed-term administrative role in a psychiatry department's general office, providing support across personnel, financial management, teaching, research, and clinical operations. Candidates require a Bachelor's degree and at least three years of relevant work experience, preferably in tertiary institutions, alongside strong organisational, interpersonal, and IT skills, with bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese and experience in financial matters or event organisation being advantageous. The role demands self-motivation, attention to detail, the ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure, and willingness to work beyond standard office hours. The position offers competitive salary, annual leave, medical benefits, and for fixed-term appointments, contract-end gratuity and retirement scheme contributions up to 10% of basic salary.

title: Research Assistant I/II (several posts)
department: Centre of Cancer Medicine, School of Clinical Medicine (22900)
description: These positions support clinical trial management within the Cancer Clinical Trials Unit, encompassing participant recruitment and screening, conducting study procedures, data collection, sample processing, documentation oversight, and quality control. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Life Sciences or related field and demonstrate strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving abilities, with fluency in English and Cantonese essential. The role requires coordinating with trial sponsors, research organizations, and internal/external partners, with opportunities for career development through initial training and ongoing cancer education. One-year appointments are available with renewal possibilities, offering competitive salaries and standard employment benefits.

title: Research Assistant I/II (several posts) in the Division of Community Medicine and Public Health Practice
description: The position involves supporting public health surveillance and research projects as a Research Assistant, with primary responsibilities including fieldwork operations, data collection and processing, electronic data management using platforms like Qualtrics and REDCap, and preparation of research reports. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in public health, biological sciences, epidemiology, or related fields, with strong English and Chinese language skills, excellent organizational abilities, and demonstrated experience in data management. The role requires independent project coordination and effective teamwork, with prior community-based research experience preferred. The appointment is temporary for one year with potential renewal, offering competitive salary and benefits.

title: 實驗室服務員
description: The School of Biomedical Sciences seeks a Laboratory Assistant on a two-year contract or one-year temporary basis, with the possibility of renewal based on satisfactory performance. Applicants must have Form 3 education or equivalent, with basic written proficiency in Chinese and English and good communication skills; prior relevant experience is preferred. The role involves cleaning offices, laboratories, and equipment, as well as handling general administrative and field duties. Starting salary is determined by educational background and work experience, with benefits including paid leave, medical coverage, and end-of-contract gratuity plus mandatory provident fund contributions (10% of total salary) for two-year contract holders.

title: Clerk I (holding the functional title of Senior Executive Assistant)
description: This two-year fixed-term Senior Executive Assistant position supports the School of Biomedical Sciences with responsibilities spanning education programme administration, academic events, alumni engagement, and general administrative duties. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree with minimum two years of work experience, demonstrates fluency in English and Chinese, and exhibits strong organizational, communication, and IT capabilities alongside self-motivation and collaborative skills. The role provides competitive compensation including retirement contributions up to 10% of basic salary, annual leave, and medical benefits, with potential for contract renewal based on performance and available funding. Applications are reviewed continuously with a deadline of April 30, 2026.

title: Post-doctoral Fellow (several posts) at the Centre for Quantitative History (CQH)
description: These are several post-doctoral positions at a research centre focused on quantitative approaches to economic, financial, and social history, with particular emphasis on China. Appointees will conduct independent research leading to academic publications, supervise research assistants, and manage centre activities including seminars and conferences. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Economics, Finance, History, or related fields completed within the past three years, with demonstrated expertise in quantitative history and fluency in both written and spoken English and Chinese. Positions are offered on one-year or two-year fixed-term contracts with competitive salaries, benefits, and potential renewal, with applications reviewed on a rolling basis until August 2026.

title: Research Assistant I/II (several posts) in the Centre for Quantitative History (CQH)
description: The Research Assistant position supports economic, financial, and social history research projects at a quantitative history centre, with responsibilities focused on data collection, compilation, and verification. Applicants should hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in economics, finance, history, or related fields, possess strong bilingual English-Chinese skills, and demonstrate proficiency in econometrics and quantitative tools such as Stata, R, GIS, or Python. The role is designed for individuals committed to PhD study in economics or economic history who value rigorous, detail-oriented research work. The temporary one-year appointment offers competitive compensation, standard benefits including medical coverage and annual leave, with renewal contingent on performance.

title: E-learning Assistant (at the rank of Research Assistant I/II)
department: Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine (20000)
description: This two-year fixed-term position supports the Faculty of Medicine's e-learning initiatives, combining educational technology expertise with content development and pedagogical support. The role involves collaborating with faculty, students, and multimedia professionals to integrate emerging technologies such as GenAI, AR/VR, and web-based tools into teaching and learning activities. Candidates should hold a postgraduate degree in biology, biomedical sciences, medicine, or related fields, with strong English proficiency and preferably prior experience in project management and e-learning applications. Web development or multimedia production skills are advantageous but not essential, and applicants with lower qualifications may be considered for the Research Assistant II tier.

title: Research Assistant I/II (holding a functional title of Full-Stack Web Developer) in the Division of Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry
department: Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry (08026)
description: This position combines full-stack web development with dental sciences research, requiring the design and maintenance of responsive web applications alongside investigation of user behavior and emerging technologies for learning management system development. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field with at least one year of professional experience in full-stack or frontend development, with proficiency in technologies including Node.js, Express.js, JavaScript, Vue.js, and PostgreSQL. Experience with LLM integrations and familiarity with learning management systems are preferred qualifications that strengthen candidacy. The role offers a temporary appointment of up to one year with renewal possibilities and includes competitive compensation, annual leave, and medical benefits.

title: Part-time Research Assistant I/II (holding a functional title of UX/UI designer) in the Division of Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry
department: Restorative Dental Sciences of the Faculty of Dentistry (08026)
description: This part-time research assistant position seeks a UX/UI designer to develop responsive websites and web applications for a dental sciences division, with emphasis on user experience, accessibility, and security. Key responsibilities include conducting user research, designing interactive interfaces, translating stakeholder requirements into functional designs, and maintaining applications across browsers. Candidates must possess a bachelor's degree in computer science or related field, at least two years of frontend development experience, and proficiency in HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and modern frameworks such as Vue.js or React, supported by a strong design portfolio. Experience with Learning Management Systems like Moodle or Blackboard, or with AI-driven feature integration, is particularly valued.

title: Senior Executive Assistant/ Executive Assistant (at the rank of Clerk I/II)
department: School of Computing and Data Science (14700)
description: This position supports a rapidly expanding computing and data science school as an Executive Assistant, requiring a Bachelor's degree, at least 3 years of work experience, and bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua). The role centers on coordinating admissions outreach, developing marketing campaigns across online and offline channels, creating promotional materials, and building engagement with the student community. The ideal candidate is a self-motivated, organized team player with admissions and student liaison experience who can manage multiple priorities independently in a fast-paced environment and work flexible hours as needed. The position is offered on a two-year fixed-term contract with competitive compensation and end-of-contract gratuity.

title: Assistant Registrar (to be seconded to GBAICTI as its Vice President (Research Strategy Development & Application))
department: Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine (20000)
description: This Assistant Registrar position is seconded to a government-backed clinical research institute as Vice President of Research Strategy Development & Application, responsible for directing research infrastructure development, managing funding schemes, and evaluating emerging methodologies while advising researchers and industry partners. The role requires a bachelor's degree or higher in medicine, pharmacy, biotechnology or related fields, plus at least 10 years of professional and supervisory experience in clinical research, with demonstrated expertise in clinical development strategies, regulatory requirements, and research infrastructure management. The position offers distinctive strategic influence in shaping clinical research policy and infrastructure across Hong Kong and the Greater Bay Area at a regional level, with direct reporting to the CEO and significant leadership responsibility for advancing research capabilities and partnerships.

title: Clerk I / II (holding the functional title of Senior Executive Assistant / Executive Assistant)
description: This temporary or fixed-term administrative role supports a School of Chinese Medicine through programme administration, event coordination, enquiry handling, and record-keeping, with flexibility to work outside standard office hours. The position requires a minimum of 5 Hong Kong secondary examination passes (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) or equivalent, plus at least 4 years of full-time work experience, alongside strong bilingual proficiency, computer literacy, and interpersonal skills. A Bachelor's degree qualifies candidates for the Executive Assistant title, with advancement to Senior Executive Assistant possible for those holding higher qualifications and more extensive experience. The role offers competitive salary and standard benefits with potential renewal contingent on performance.

title: Part-time Instructors (Part-time Programmes –General Courses and other Professional Continuing Education Programmes)
department: School of Continuing and Professional Studies
description: Instructors in this position teach general and professional continuing education courses within part-time programmes, delivering instruction to adult and non-traditional learners outside standard full-time academic schedules. Key requirements include subject matter expertise in assigned course areas and demonstrated ability to engage diverse student populations in continuing education contexts. This role is distinctive in its focus on professional development and skill-building for working professionals and career-changers rather than traditional degree-seeking students. The position operates on a part-time basis, offering flexibility while contributing to the school's mission of providing accessible, practical education and training programmes.

title: Research Assistant Professor
department: Department of Geography and Resource Management
description: This Research Assistant Professor position focuses on wetland ecology and biogeochemistry, specifically investigating coastal wetlands as climate solutions. Candidates must hold a PhD in physical geography, environmental science, or related fields, with strong expertise in biogeochemistry, ecology, soil science, or geospatial analysis, along with a proven publication record in top-tier journals and excellent communication skills. The role involves conducting independent and collaborative research, securing external funding as Principal Investigator, publishing peer-reviewed work, and contributing to teaching and student supervision. The appointment is contract-based for up to three years, renewable based on performance and funding availability.

title: Lecturer
description: This Lecturer position in the Office of University General Education teaches courses within a China-focused general education programme, including "Understanding China" and "Hong Kong in the Wider Constitutional Order," while contributing to curriculum development and student enrichment activities. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Humanities or Social Sciences with demonstrated expertise in China studies, though those with backgrounds in economic development or international relations are particularly encouraged. The role emphasizes teaching excellence and knowledge advancement, with prior research on China topics, fieldwork experience, or teaching experience in China studies considered advantageous. The position is offered on an initial one- to two-year contract beginning August 2026 or January 2027, with renewal possible based on performance.

title: Associate Professor/ Assistant Professor
department: Department of Earth and Environmental Sciences
description: The department seeks tenure-track faculty at the Assistant or Associate Professor level to strengthen research and teaching in Earth and Environmental Sciences, particularly in atmospheric and climate sciences, biogeosciences, geophysics, and aquatic sciences. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. (postdoctoral experience preferred) and demonstrate excellent teaching and research potential, with strong preference for expertise in data analytics, machine learning, computing, and quantitative modeling. Appointees will be responsible for establishing a well-funded independent research program while contributing to undergraduate and graduate instruction across core and interdisciplinary courses. Initial three-year contracts beginning August 2026 may lead to longer-term positions, and the department actively encourages applications from women and underrepresented minorities.

title: Professor/ Associate Professor and Chairperson, Department of Educational Administration and Policy
department: Department of Educational Administration and Policy
description: This position seeks a distinguished scholar to lead the Department of Educational Administration and Policy as Chairperson at Professor or Associate Professor level, with appointment on a tenured or contract basis. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in educational administration, leadership, policy studies, or related disciplines, with proven leadership experience in higher education, a strong research record, and demonstrated excellence in teaching and scholarship. The role requires providing visionary leadership to develop departmental strategy aligned with institutional goals, fostering a culture of research excellence through interdisciplinary collaboration, delivering high-quality teaching across all programme levels, and building strategic partnerships with leading institutions. Applicants should submit an online application with a full curriculum vitae and three referee details, with unsuccessful applicants likely to be notified within six months.

description: The department seeks two tenure-track Assistant Professors in theoretical or experimental physics, with exceptional candidates considered for senior positions. Applicants must hold a PhD with postdoctoral experience, demonstrate established research accomplishments and capacity to secure external funding, and show commitment to teaching at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels. Initial contracts typically last three years, with potential for longer-term or permanent appointment upon mutual agreement. Candidates should submit applications online including a resume, research and teaching statements, credentials, publications, and three referees' details.

title: Research Assistant Professors
description: The Department of Mathematics seeks promising early-career mathematicians for non-substantiable Research Assistant Professor positions across all mathematical fields, with preference for candidates whose research aligns with existing faculty expertise. Applicants must hold a PhD in mathematics or related disciplines and demonstrate strong research potential and teaching capability. The role emphasizes research with light teaching responsibilities (one course annually at undergraduate or postgraduate level), offered on a three-year renewable contract beginning August 2026. Applications should include a resume, academic credentials, and contact details for at least four referees (three research-focused, one teaching-focused), with review commencing December 1, 2025.

title: Professors/ Associate Professors/ Assistant Professors
description: This position offers tenure-track faculty appointments at the Professor, Associate Professor, or Assistant Professor level in the Department of Mathematics, with potential joint affiliation at the Institute of Mathematical Sciences. Candidates must hold a PhD in mathematics or a related field and demonstrate an established research record with publications alongside a commitment to teaching excellence. The initial appointment is contract-based for up to three years beginning August 2026, with the possibility of substantiation or longer-term employment thereafter. The department is highly internationally ranked and seeks to recruit accomplished scholars to strengthen its reputation in both research and education.

title: Lecturer
description: A globally-ranked sociology department seeks a Lecturer with a doctoral degree in sociology and demonstrated teaching experience, particularly in digital society, artificial intelligence, media and culture, or related subfields such as technology, work and organisations, or sociological theory. The successful candidate will teach at undergraduate and postgraduate levels while contributing to departmental development initiatives and community engagement. The fixed-term position, renewable based on performance, begins in August 2026 and offers an opportunity to join a diverse, internationally-recognised department ranked 18th globally.

Key requirements include a proven ability to foster interdisciplinary work and experience developing study programmes, with particular value placed on expertise relating to China's role in global governance and cross-cultural competence in Chinese and Western contexts. The position is offered on an initial three-year contract beginning January 2026, with potential for longer-term appointment or tenure based on performance and mutual agreement.

Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in educational administration, leadership, or a related field, demonstrate a strong research track record with evidence of programmatic research and publications, and show exceptional commitment to both teaching and scholarship; at the senior levels, applicants must exhibit established standing and international professional leadership in their field. Appointees will teach across undergraduate and postgraduate levels, supervise doctoral students, pursue external research funding, and may assume concurrent leadership roles in faculty research centers. Positions are initially offered on three-year contracts that may lead to longer-term or permanent appointment.

department: Department of Educational Administration and Policy

Ideal candidates possess a doctoral degree in related fields such as policy studies, philosophy of education, or artificial intelligence in education, with strong research records, publications, and demonstrated potential for obtaining competitive grants. Appointees will teach at undergraduate and postgraduate levels, supervise graduate students, lead research initiatives, and secure external funding while contributing to departmental administration. This is a contract-based position (up to three years initially) that may progress to longer-term appointment or substantiation based on funding and performance.

For Associate and Professor positions, candidates with industry experience, programme development expertise, grant-winning track records, and academic leadership are preferred. Assistant Professor appointments are initially contract-based for up to three years starting August 2026, with potential for longer-term appointment, while exceptional senior candidates may be considered for immediate substantive positions.

The position is offered on a three-year contract basis.

title: Director, School of Hotel & Tourism Management, CUHK Business School
department: School of Hotel and Tourism Management
description: This role seeks a visionary director to lead the School of Hotel & Tourism Management within a prestigious business school, reporting to the Dean and serving on the leadership team with overall responsibility for academic and administrative oversight. The ideal candidate must demonstrate scholarly excellence and a strong publication record in hospitality and/or real estate, hold a doctoral degree in related business disciplines, and possess substantial experience in teaching and research at research universities or tertiary institutions. Key requirements also include prior administrative experience in higher education or industry, strong communication and networking abilities, and established connections within hospitality and real estate sectors, with international experience highly valued. The appointment is fixed-term for three years and emphasizes the need for a collaborative leader capable of enhancing programme quality, advancing research, and strengthening business community engagement.

title: Project Assistant II
description: This Project Assistant II position supports a residential college's administrative operations across student activities, events, admissions, and financial matters, requiring clerical work, logistical coordination, and general office duties with occasional irregular hours. Candidates must have completed secondary education with specific language qualifications in English and Chinese, a Bachelor's degree (preferably in Accounting), at least 2 years of work experience (ideally in tertiary institutions), and strong proficiency in Microsoft Office and bilingual communication. The role particularly values experience in student activity coordination, self-motivation, responsibility, and the ability to work independently while managing multiple administrative functions. The position is offered initially on a 2-year renewable contract basis.

title: Project Co-ordinator II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Manager, Student Services’)
description: This position supports a cooperative education programme that combines undergraduate classroom learning with practical work experience. The role encompasses student recruitment and outreach, academic administration (including timetabling and course evaluation), event organisation, marketing and communications, and general office management. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree with at least three years' work experience, ideally in higher education, along with excellent written and spoken English and Chinese, strong organisational and communication skills, and proficiency in event management. The role is offered on an initial two-year contract with potential renewal based on performance.

title: Executive Officer I / II (carrying the job title of ‘Senior Manager / Manager (Academic and Resources Planning)’), Office of EMBA (Chinese) Programme
description: The position is an Executive Officer (Senior/Manager level) in the Office of an EMBA (Chinese) Programme within a leading business school, responsible for comprehensive academic and resource management of a selective part-time programme serving working professionals across Hong Kong and mainland China. Key responsibilities include overseeing academic matters and student support throughout their learning journey, managing curriculum development and quality assurance, handling budget and resource allocation, leading programme staff across locations, and maintaining stakeholder relationships. The role requires a Bachelor's degree (Master's preferred) ideally in business or related fields, with fluency in English, and involves flexible working hours and occasional travel outside Hong Kong. The position offers the opportunity to support a prestigious postgraduate programme with a strong regional reputation while managing both academic excellence and operational efficiency.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This role supports academic programmes within an engineering department's administrative office, requiring the coordinator to manage admissions, curricula, and student matters; organize departmental events; maintain digital communications platforms; and produce documentation and reports. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree with at least two years of work experience preferably in higher education, combined with fluency in English and Chinese, strong computer skills (Microsoft Office and photo editing software), and the ability to work independently under pressure while managing multiple tasks. Key personal qualities include excellent interpersonal communication, organizational skills, attention to detail, and team orientation. The position is offered on an initial two-year renewable contract, with shortlisted applicants required to complete a written assessment.

title: Computer Technician II (carrying the job title of ‘IT Infrastructure Administrator, SCS’)
department: School of Continuing and Professional Studies
description: This IT Infrastructure Administrator position in the School of Continuing and Professional Studies involves managing network infrastructure, security devices, and server systems at the Tseung Kwan O Learning Centre, while providing technical support and ensuring system reliability through backup and disaster recovery operations. The ideal candidate will hold a diploma or bachelor's degree in Information Technology and possess at least 5 years of relevant experience in infrastructure and network engineering, with demonstrated expertise in L2/L3 networking, Windows/Linux systems administration, and enterprise security controls. Key responsibilities include optimizing network performance, administering virtualization platforms, managing storage solutions, and collaborating with internal teams and external vendors on IT projects. The position is offered on a contract basis for up to 2 years (renewable based on performance), with candidates required to pass written and technical skills assessments.

title: Project Co-ordinator II, Marketing and Admissions, Office of MBA Programmes
description: This role coordinates the end-to-end MBA admission process for a leading business school, managing interview scheduling, document vetting, admissions records, and liaison with faculty and university departments. Key responsibilities include providing administrative support to the marketing and admissions team, preparing admission statistics and reports, analyzing regional market trends, managing scholarships, and supporting general office operations including financial processing and logistics. The position requires a bachelor's degree, at least two years of post-qualification executive experience (preferably in tertiary education), strong organizational and team-working skills, proficiency in Microsoft Office and Chinese, and flexibility to work weekends during recruitment periods. The role is distinctive for its combination of operational admissions management with strategic intelligence analysis and its emphasis on working within a collaborative, mission-driven programme office culture.

title: Full-time/Part-time Nurse
department: Department of Anaesthesia and Intensive Care
description: The appointee will support multiple clinical research projects in intensive care across three affiliated hospital sites in Hong Kong, with primary duties at North District Hospital. Key responsibilities include coordinating research studies, managing ethics applications, obtaining patient consent, collecting biological samples, administering study medications, recruiting participants, and performing data analysis and health education. The ideal candidate is a registered nurse with a valid Hong Kong Practising Certificate and preferably has experience in clinical research, particularly in sample collection, medication administration, and database management. The position is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract.

title: Assistant Computer Officer (carrying the job title of ‘Manager of Information Technology Services, SCS’)
department: School of Continuing and Professional Studies
