description: This position is for an Assistant Computer Officer at a School of Continuing and Professional Studies, responsible for leading the full application development lifecycle, overseeing database administration, and managing IT support operations across in-house and enterprise systems. The role requires a Bachelor's degree in IT or Computer Science with at least 8 years of relevant experience in application development, system integration, and IT project management, preferably in an educational setting. Key technical competencies include hands-on proficiency in full-stack development (Java, PHP, JavaScript, Python), database administration for both relational and NoSQL systems, and emerging technologies like AI. The appointee will manage a team of IT developers, collaborate with business stakeholders to translate requirements into technical solutions, and coordinate with external vendors to ensure quality project delivery within a self-financing continuing education institution.

title: Nurse
description: This nursing position in the surgery department involves supporting clinical research through project coordination, subject recruitment, data collection, and administrative tasks. Candidates must be registered nurses with valid practising certificates, hold a bachelor's or master's degree in biological or medical sciences, and demonstrate proficiency in English and Chinese alongside strong computer skills in Microsoft Office and statistical software. Prior experience in clinical research support or public communication is advantageous. The role offers an initial one-year contract renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Executive Officer II
department: Hong Kong Institute of Educational Research
description: This position provides executive and administrative support to Programme Co-Directors managing self-financed and in-service teacher education programmes, with responsibilities spanning programme promotion, admissions, scheduling, budgeting, event coordination, student services, quality assurance, and proposal development. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree, demonstrate proficiency in English and Chinese (both written and spoken), possess at least 5 years of post-qualification executive experience, and show advanced MS Office skills alongside strong communication, organisational, and problem-solving abilities. The role demands meticulous attention to detail and a high sense of responsibility, with occasional work required outside regular office hours. Applicants must submit certificates verifying language and academic qualifications to be considered, and shortlisted candidates will complete a written test; the position is initially offered on a renewable 1-year contract.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
department: The Jockey Club School of Public Health and Primary Care
description: This position is a Project Coordinator II role supporting a university-based collaborating centre focused on disaster and medical humanitarian response, responsible for providing executive support across administration, finance, and personnel management, while also preparing reports, maintaining the centre's website, and coordinating events and delegations. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree with at least two years of post-qualification project management experience, preferably in tertiary institutions, along with fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua) and proficiency in computer applications. The role requires strong analytical, organisational, and problem-solving skills, demonstrated integrity, and the ability to work independently and meet tight deadlines, with prior conference organisation experience being advantageous. The position is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Office Assistant
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This Office Assistant role supports daily operations at a university pathology service laboratory, involving administrative tasks such as faxing, report handling, record scanning, data entry, and specimen delivery. The position requires Form 3 education as a minimum, strong organizational and communication skills, proficiency in English and Cantonese, and competency with standard office software including MS Word and Excel. The role emphasizes responsibility, attention to detail, and teamwork, with candidates expected to work independently across both office-based and outdoor duties. The appointment is initially for one year on a renewable contract, based at a hospital laboratory facility.

title: Project Assistant II
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This Project Assistant II position involves delivering administrative and operational support to academic programmes, managing payments and inventory, providing frontline office services, and supporting on-site classes and events, with flexibility for non-standard hours including weekends. Candidates must have completed secondary education with specified qualifications in Chinese and English, at least one year of work experience (preferably in tertiary institutions), and demonstrated proficiency in MS Office applications and bilingual communication in English, Chinese, Cantonese, and Putonghua. Key competencies include fast typing skills, strong organisational and interpersonal abilities, and the capacity to work both independently and collaboratively, with shortlisted applicants required to pass a computer skills test. The appointment is initially for one year on a renewable contract basis contingent on performance.

title: Technician
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This technician position in the molecular laboratory requires a Bachelor's degree in Biological Sciences or Biomedical Sciences and practical experience with molecular techniques including DNA/RNA extraction, PCR, Sanger sequencing, and NGS, with histological techniques being advantageous. The role is initially offered on a fixed-term contract of up to two years with the possibility of renewal based on performance and mutual agreement. The position is distinctive for its focus on advanced molecular diagnostics within a pathology setting at a hospital laboratory. Applicants must submit their applications online through the institutional career portal.

title: Medical Laboratory Technician II [Molecular Diagnostic Laboratory (Histopathology)]
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This position involves technical laboratory work in a molecular diagnostic histopathology unit, requiring a Bachelor's degree or Diploma in Medical Laboratory Sciences and professional registration as a Medical Laboratory Technologist with a valid practising certificate in Hong Kong. The role offers an advantage to candidates with prior experience in histopathology or molecular laboratory settings. The appointment is initially for one year on a renewable contract, contingent on satisfactory performance and mutual agreement.

title: Assistant Officer (at the rank of Project Assistant II)
description: The Department of Medicine and Therapeutics seeks a Project Assistant II (Assistant Officer) to support academic administration, provide secretarial services to clinical staff, and assist with clinical examinations and programme delivery at a hospital-based office. Candidates require completion of secondary education (preferably an Associate Degree or higher), passes in at least five HKCEE/HKDSE subjects including Chinese and English, strong typing skills, fluency in written and spoken English and Putonghua, and proficiency in MS Office. The role demands independent problem-solving, attention to detail, and responsibility, with prior experience in healthcare or educational clerical support preferred. Appointment is offered on an initial two-year contract with renewable terms based on performance.

title: Research Assistant (carrying the job title of 'AI Research Assistant')
department: Department of Systems Engineering and Engineering Management
description: This position supports an AI education initiative in collaboration with MIT, aimed at building AI literacy among Hong Kong students through equitable access to quality learning resources. The Research Assistant will design and deliver AI training materials and workshops for both teachers and students, analyze school needs for AI implementation, and develop engaging educational content tailored to diverse audiences. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree (preferably in AI, Computer Science, Engineering, Education, or related fields), demonstrate strong understanding of AI concepts in educational contexts, possess excellent communication skills, and be fluent in Cantonese, English, and Putonghua. Teaching experience, advanced AI knowledge, programming proficiency, and experience developing AI models are desirable but not required; the role is offered on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position in a nursing school seeks candidates with a PhD in health-related fields (particularly health psychology, public health, or social sciences) who have research experience with marginalised populations, especially youth and older adults. Core responsibilities include designing and conducting research projects, managing data collection and analysis, and contributing to grant proposals and academic publications. Applicants must demonstrate strong quantitative and/or qualitative research skills, a publication record, and fluency in both English and Chinese. The position is initially offered for one year on a contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports the Maternal Fetal Medicine Unit at a hospital-based laboratory, requiring candidates to assist with clinical subject recruitment, data collection, laboratory research, and sample handling across multiple sites. Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree in Biology, Biochemistry, or related field, with demonstrated clinical laboratory experience, proficiency in Microsoft applications and SPSS, and fluency in English and Chinese (including Cantonese and Putonghua). The role emphasizes independent work capability and strong communication skills, with preference given to candidates with accredited laboratory experience. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Assistant Computer Officer
description: This role involves developing, implementing, and maintaining information security programmes for an internet exchange service provider. Key responsibilities include administering security systems, managing incidents, establishing security policies, and coordinating with external vendors. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree in a relevant technical field with at least two years' experience in information security, demonstrating proficiency in cybersecurity technologies such as firewalls, SIEM, and penetration testing, alongside strong knowledge of networking protocols and ITIL frameworks. The position offers a two-year renewable contract and prefers candidates holding professional certifications such as CISSP or CISA.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
department: The Jockey Club School of Public Health and Primary Care
description: This position offers a research role at two levels—Research Associate (requiring a Master's degree and four years of relevant experience) or Research Assistant (Bachelor's degree sufficient)—within a public health and primary care department, specifically supporting a collaborating centre focused on disaster and medical humanitarian response. Candidates must demonstrate competency in quantitative or qualitative data analysis, strong organizational and communication abilities, and fluency in English and Chinese, with preference given to those with experience in humanitarian medicine or health emergency management. Primary responsibilities include conducting independent research activities such as systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and data analysis; writing grant proposals and manuscripts; and managing research projects and datasets. The position is offered on a fixed-term contract of up to one year, renewable based on performance.

title: Technician
description: This technician position supports the Orthopaedic Learning Centre and Department at a hospital, with responsibilities including cadaver training coordination, workshop setup, equipment maintenance, website management, and general administrative duties, often requiring work beyond standard hours. Candidates must hold a diploma or certificate in biological sciences or related fields with at least three years of relevant experience, plus proficiency in computer applications, bilingual communication in Chinese and English, and strong organizational and teamwork abilities. Preferred qualifications include experience with audio-visual systems and medical instruments. The role is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract contingent on performance.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position supports ongoing research in paediatric practices, bioethics, and public education at a children's hospital, requiring a Bachelor's degree in social sciences, healthcare, education, or related fields, with strong communication, organizational, and bilingual (Chinese-English) computer skills. Key responsibilities include coordinating development of an ethics education course for medical students, conducting clinical research involving patient recruitment and questionnaires, managing data entry, and assisting with ethics applications and administrative documentation. Prior experience as a Research Assistant in clinical settings is preferred but not required, with consideration given to candidates with less experience for the junior role. The contract-based position is offered initially for one year, renewable by mutual agreement, with on-the-job training provided.

title: Project Assistant II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Programme Officer’)
description: The Office of University General Education seeks a Project Assistant II to provide clerical and operational support for General Education programmes, including data management, student services, event organisation, office administration, and procurement support. Candidates must have completed secondary education with Grade E or above in at least 5 HKCEE/HKDSE subjects (including English and Chinese), along with proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and strong bilingual (English/Chinese) communication skills. Essential qualities include attention to detail, responsibility, initiative, good interpersonal abilities, and teamwork, with data processing experience preferred; applicants will be required to sit a written test. The position is initially offered on a two-year renewable contract basis, with applications submitted exclusively through the university's online career portal.

title: Assistant Officer (at the rank of Project Assistant II)
description: The Alumni Affairs Office seeks an Assistant Officer to support alumni engagement and community building through event logistics, digital communications, and administrative tasks. Candidates must have completed secondary education with strong results in Chinese and English, alongside excellent word-processing abilities, fluent bilingual communication skills, and proficiency in IT, particularly in digital communication and graphic design. Prior experience in tertiary institutions or skills in digital marketing, website development, or database management are advantageous. The role is offered initially on a 2-year renewable contract, with shortlisted candidates required to pass written and computer skills assessments.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: The Physical Education Unit seeks a Project Co-ordinator II to manage financial operations, including expenditure applications and budget monitoring, oversee human resources support such as recruitment and onboarding, and handle procurement and purchases according to institutional policies. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree with at least two years of administrative experience, demonstrate proficiency in MS Office and bilingual competency in English and Chinese, and possess strong organisational, communication, and analytical skills alongside self-motivation and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. The role is initially offered on a two-year renewable contract with contract-end gratuity, contingent on satisfactory performance.

title: Project Assistant II
description: This Project Assistant II role supports laboratory operations through procurement and financial document handling, event coordination, database maintenance, and general clerical duties. Candidates must have completed secondary education with specified qualifications in Chinese and English, plus at least one year of work experience and proficiency in word processing, MS Office, and spoken English and Chinese. Strong organizational skills, responsibility, and self-motivation are essential, with graphic design experience preferred. The position offers an initial one-year contract renewable based on performance, with applicants required to submit certificates demonstrating academic qualifications and language competency alongside their CV.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
department: Office for Greater Bay Area Developments
description: This Project Co-ordinator II position supports cross-border initiatives between the University and its subsidiaries in the greater bay area by managing project execution, coordinating communications among diverse stakeholders, and providing administrative and logistical support. The role requires a bachelor's degree and at least 3 years of relevant experience, with essential skills including fluency in English and Chinese (Putonghua), strong organisational and interpersonal abilities, event planning expertise, and proficiency in standard office software. Candidates should demonstrate high responsibility and motivation, with prior experience in Chinese Mainland projects being advantageous. The position is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract with gratuity.

title: Executive Officer II
description: This Executive Officer II position involves administering scholarships and financial aid, managing student admission and recruitment activities, coordinating student development and alumni programmes, and organizing college events and workshops, along with secretarial duties. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree with at least 4 years of relevant executive experience (preferably in tertiary institutions or public organisations), demonstrates fluency in English and Chinese (including Cantonese and Putonghua), and possesses strong organisational, interpersonal, and problem-solving abilities. Applicants must submit certified copies of academic credentials demonstrating language proficiency and qualifications, as applications without these documents will not be considered. The role is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract with contract-end gratuity, contingent on strong performance.

title: Project Co-ordinator II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant College Development Officer’)
description: The position oversees residential student services, including managing hostels and staff, while also coordinating major social events such as college banquets and orientation programs. Key responsibilities encompass administrative support for multiple college committees, organisation of cultural activities, management of residential and visitor accommodations, and secretarial duties. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree with fluent English and Chinese proficiency, strong organisational and interpersonal skills, and the ability to multitask under pressure, with flexibility for work beyond standard office hours. The role is offered on an initial two-year renewable contract.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This Project Co-ordinator II role in the international relations office involves implementing a summer research programme for international students, administering research grants and mobility schemes, and supporting partnership development and global engagement initiatives including event organisation and communications. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree and has at least two years of relevant work experience, preferably in a tertiary or international English-speaking environment, with strong organisational and communication skills, proficiency in Microsoft Office, and experience managing both physical and virtual events. Essential qualities include fluency in spoken and written English, cultural awareness, teamwork ability, and the capacity to work independently in a fast-paced setting, with Chinese language proficiency considered advantageous. The position is offered on a renewable two-year contract with performance-based renewal and contract-end gratuity.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This Project Co-ordinator II position supports the Centre for Innovation and Technology within the Faculty of Engineering, serving as secretary to the Centre Director while managing stakeholder communications, editing publications and promotional materials, and coordinating events and marketing activities. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Marketing, Communications, Public Relations, or related fields, with at least three years of post-qualification professional experience in marketing or corporate communications. Essential qualifications include fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese (Putonghua), along with strong interpersonal, communication, analytical, and organisational skills, with demonstrated ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively across diverse stakeholder groups. The role is offered on an initial two-year contract, renewable based on performance, with prior experience in tertiary institutions considered advantageous.

title: Executive Officer II
department: Department of Otorhinolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery
description: This Executive Officer II position supports the Division of Speech Therapy within the Department of Otorhinolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery, managing postgraduate and professional diploma programmes while overseeing divisional administration including budgeting, human resources, and project management. Key responsibilities include assisting programme directors, coordinating academic events and seminars, providing meeting support, and supervising junior staff. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree with at least three years of academic administration or project coordination experience, demonstrates bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (including Putonghua), and possesses strong organisational, analytical, and interpersonal skills alongside proficiency in MS Office applications. The position is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written test as part of the selection process.

title: Clerical Assistant
description: This clerical assistant position in the Estates Management Office involves providing administrative and operational support, maintaining records, responding to inquiries, and performing related duties as needed. Candidates must have completed secondary education, strong word-processing and MS Office skills, fluency in English and Chinese, meticulous attention to detail, and excellent communication abilities, with SAP system experience being advantageous. The role includes a computer skills assessment for shortlisted applicants. The position is offered on an initial two-year renewable contract with performance-based continuation.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This Project Co-ordinator II role supports a residential college within a university, managing admissions, scholarships, financial aid, and exchange programmes while coordinating student activities and serving as secretary to college committees. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree or higher with at least three years of post-qualification experience, ideally in tertiary education, and must demonstrate strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, computer literacy, and excellent interpersonal skills. Prior experience with student admissions, scholarships, or college exchange programmes is highly valued. The position requires occasional work outside standard office hours and is initially offered on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
department: Centre for the Studies of Daoist Culture
description: This position supports a specialized academic centre by managing administrative and financial operations, coordinating research programs, conferences, and public lectures, and providing secretarial assistance to senior leadership. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Chinese Culture or related fields with at least two years of administrative experience, ideally in higher education, alongside fluency in English and Chinese and strong proficiency with computer applications. The role requires independent working capabilities, ability to handle pressure, excellent organizational and interpersonal skills, with prior research project coordination experience being advantageous. The appointment is initially for two years on a renewable contract basis, contingent on performance.

title: Project Assistant II
description: This position supports the Department of Psychiatry's administrative and operational functions, including managing student activities, organizing academic events and conferences, updating digital platforms, and compiling departmental information. The role requires secondary education completion with specified passes in Chinese and English, proficiency in MS Office applications, strong bilingual communication skills, and the ability to work independently under pressure, with shortlisted candidates subject to an IT test. The appointee will primarily work at Tai Po Hospital with occasional assignments at other locations, employed initially on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Senior Editor
description: This senior editorial role supports the Communications and Public Relations Office in driving strategic content creation aligned with institutional priorities, including overseeing content coordination, writing, editing, and stakeholder engagement. The position requires a bachelor's degree (master's preferred) and at least 12 years of post-qualification editorial experience in media or publications, alongside fluent written and spoken English and Chinese including Putonghua. Candidates must demonstrate strong multi-stakeholder communication skills and knowledge of higher education and the public sector in Hong Kong and mainland China, with journalism or communication discipline experience preferred. The appointment is a 2-year renewable contract with written assessments conducted in both Chinese and English.

title: Research Associate(s)
department: Department of Systems Engineering and Engineering Management
description: This Research Associate position supports an AI education initiative aimed at developing AI literacy among Hong Kong students through collaboration with schools and academic partners. Candidates should hold a Master's degree in a related field with at least four years of relevant experience, along with strong communication skills, proficiency in Cantonese, English, and Putonghua, and demonstrated enthusiasm for AI education. Key responsibilities include designing and publishing AI training materials, delivering workshops to students and teachers, conducting needs assessments for schools, and supporting curriculum development initiatives. Desirable qualifications include teaching experience, programming skills, and knowledge of advanced AI concepts, with the position offered initially on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Works Supervisor I/II (Builder Works) (carrying the job title of ‘Maintenance Officer’)
description: The position involves supervising building maintenance and renovation projects, leading internal teams through site inspections and technical work, and managing contractor activities across multiple worksites. Candidates must hold a diploma in Building Studies or equivalent qualification plus at least three years of supervisory experience in builder works and staff management, or alternatively possess five years of relevant site supervision experience with strong academic credentials. Knowledge of facilities management software and a valid driving licence are required or preferred. The role is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract basis, with flexibility for shift work, overtime, and weekend availability as needed.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: Research Assistants or Junior Research Assistants in the Department of Medicine and Therapeutics will conduct translational research on age-related neurodegenerative diseases at a hospital-based neuroscience laboratory, performing molecular assays (Western blot, ELISA, qPCR), cell culture, histology, and in vivo experimentation alongside routine laboratory maintenance. Candidates should hold a Master's or Bachelor's degree in Biological Science or related fields with at least one year of relevant post-qualification experience, strong communication and problem-solving skills, and preferably expertise in molecular assays, in vivo models (MCAO, stereotactic injection, behavioral testing), or programming. The position is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Project Assistant II
department: Minor Programme in Entrepreneurship and Innovation
description: This Project Assistant II role supports an interdisciplinary entrepreneurship and innovation programme by managing social media and website content, coordinating administrative and logistical functions for courses and events, liaising with internal and external partners, and providing clerical support for meetings. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree (or secondary education minimum) with specified Hong Kong qualification standards in English and Chinese, along with strong proficiency in MS Office applications and excellent written and spoken bilingual communication skills. Familiarity with graphic design tools such as Adobe Creative Cloud is advantageous, and prior administrative experience is preferred, though fresh graduates are encouraged to apply. The position is offered on an initial two-year contract with the possibility of renewal based on performance.

title: Executive Officer II [carrying the job title of 'Visitor Centre Development Specialist' (Project‑Based)]
description: This project-based role requires an experienced professional to lead the complete redevelopment of a visitor centre, overseeing all stages from concept and design through construction to operation and sustainability. The position demands responsibility for project lifecycle management, stakeholder collaboration with consultants and contractors, budget and cost control, and developing a viable business model encompassing retail operations, product offerings, and omnichannel customer experience. The successful candidate must hold a Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, Interior Design, or related field with at least 10 years of proven experience in retail operations, product development, or brand management, plus demonstrated expertise in managing renovation projects and understanding brand strategy and customer experience design. This distinctive opportunity combines strategic institutional responsibility with hands-on operational management to create a vibrant community engagement space that reflects institutional values while achieving commercial viability.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
description: This Project Co-ordinator II position in the Faculty of Science oversees undergraduate admissions across all channels, including interview coordination, document validation, offer processing, and applicant engagement, alongside supporting faculty events and managing promotional materials and social media. The role requires a bachelor's degree, at least two years of administrative experience (preferably in higher education), strong proficiency in Microsoft 365 and other digital tools, and fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua). Essential competencies include meticulous attention to detail, excellent organisational and interpersonal skills, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively, with graphic design experience being advantageous. The position is offered on an initial two-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Scientific Officer
description: This Scientific Officer position in the Department of Surgery involves conducting research on innovative RIBOTAC and PROTAC therapeutics, managing laboratory operations, and supervising research staff and students while preparing publications and grant proposals. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biomedical or Analytical Chemistry with at least 2 years of postdoctoral research experience, strong knowledge of Molecular Biology and Analytical Chemistry, and a solid publication record, with laboratory management experience being advantageous. The role is initially offered on a 2-year renewable contract basis with performance-dependent continuation. Applications must be submitted online through the institutional career portal.

title: Clerical Assistant
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This clerical assistant position supports the University Pathology Service at a hospital laboratory, handling daily administrative operations including document management, data entry, specimen tracking, and report processing. The role requires secondary education completion and strong bilingual (English/Chinese) skills in word processing, MS Office applications, and verbal communication. Key attributes include meticulous attention to detail, responsibility, self-motivation, teamwork ability, and capacity to work independently, with duties also encompassing specimen and report delivery within and outside the hospital. The appointment is initially for one year and renewable based on performance.

title: Accountant II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Finance Manager’)
description: This role manages financial controls and budgets for various institutional accounts including donations, grants, and external funds while supervising daily accounting operations and coordinating financial reporting and analysis. The successful candidate must hold a Bachelor's degree and professional accounting qualification (HKICPA, ACCA, CA or equivalent) with at least four years of post-qualification experience in auditing and accounting, including full bookkeeping experience. Key requirements include strong analytical and organisational abilities, proficiency in MS Office and both English and Chinese languages, and the capacity to work independently and meet tight deadlines, with SAP and PeopleSoft experience being advantageous. The position is distinctive in its dual focus on financial oversight and internal control development, offered initially on a two-year renewable contract with gratuity.

title: Executive Officer II (Innovation and Impact Development)
description: This Knowledge Transfer Office position involves managing strategic innovation initiatives through stakeholder liaison, administering major funding programmes from planning through reporting, providing high-level support to committees, and preparing complex reports and proposals. The role requires at least two years of executive experience, preferably in grant management and stakeholder negotiation, alongside fluency in English and Chinese and strong organisational and analytical capabilities. Key qualifications include a Bachelor's degree or higher, excellent communication and problem-solving skills, and proficiency in multitasking under pressure, with particular value placed on report-writing and funding-related experience. The position is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written assessment.

title: Project Assistant II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Programme Officer’)
description: This position supports graduate programme administration, focusing on admissions, registration, student data processing, and general postgraduate office duties. Candidates must have secondary education completion with specified passes in Chinese and English, at least two years of administrative experience (preferably in higher education), strong typing and computer skills, and fluency in English, Chinese, and Putonghua. The role requires meticulous attention to detail, analytical ability, and the capacity to manage tight deadlines, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written assessment. The appointment is initially contract-based for up to two years with potential renewal based on performance.

title: Part-time Nurse
description: This part-time nursing role involves supporting research operations through administrative coordination, data collection and management, literature review, and manuscript preparation. Candidates must be registered nurses in Hong Kong with a valid Practising Certificate and at least three years of post-registration clinical experience, along with fluent bilingual communication skills (English and Cantonese) and strong computer proficiency. The position requires the ability to work independently, multitask effectively, and demonstrate high responsibility and self-motivation. The appointment is contract-based for up to two months, with applicants required to submit a CV and academic credentials demonstrating their professional qualifications.

title: Project Assistant II (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Programme Administrator’)
description: The role involves providing administrative and clerical support to postgraduate nursing programs, including assisting students with admissions enquiries, coordinating program events, and handling general administrative duties. Applicants must have completed secondary education with strong qualifications (Grade E or equivalent in at least 5 subjects including Chinese and English), at least 2 years of relevant program administration experience, and proficiency in word processing and standard computer applications. The position requires strong written and spoken English and Chinese language skills. This is a 2-year fixed contract position with gratuity, renewable based on performance.

title: Part-time Project Co-ordinator II (carrying the job title of 'Promotion Officer')
description: This part-time position coordinates a research project on end-of-life care for people with intellectual disabilities, requiring the incumbent to manage clerical and data support, organize promotional activities, create social media content, and assist with video production. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree, demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese, and have at least three years of post-qualification experience, ideally in marketing or event organization. The role demands strong communication, interpersonal, and time management skills, with the ability to work independently and manage expectations across internal and external stakeholders. The appointment is initially for one year and renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position supports research at the intersection of generative AI and architectural heritage, focusing on Lingnan water-adaptive architecture through multi-scalar morphological analysis. The Research Assistant will conduct heritage analysis, develop specialized image datasets with performance-based captioning, fine-tune AI models, and coordinate broader research activities including publications and workshops. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in Architecture, Urban Design, or Data Science with proficiency in GIS and Adobe Suite; experience with generative AI tools and machine learning is highly desirable. The role is initially a three-month renewable contract, with exceptional candidates considered for doctoral study opportunities.

title: Laboratory Assistant
description: This Laboratory Assistant position in the nursing school involves preparing clinical skills equipment, maintaining the simulation centre, supporting school events, and performing administrative tasks like record-keeping and data entry. Candidates must have completed secondary education with specific qualifications in Chinese and English plus three additional subjects, basic computer proficiency, strong interpersonal skills, and the ability to work independently; prior experience with nursing equipment is advantageous. The role is distinctive for combining technical laboratory support with administrative responsibilities in a clinical education setting. Initial appointment is for one year, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Educational Administration and Policy
description: The position is a Research Assistant supporting a project investigating school networks and system leadership across selected schools in the Greater Bay Area. Candidates must hold a bachelor's degree or higher in Education or Social Sciences with at least one year of research experience in educational contexts, along with fluent English and Chinese language skills and proficiency in survey design, interviews, and data analysis. Key responsibilities include conducting literature reviews, collecting and analyzing data, writing reports, and supporting the Project Leader as needed. The role is offered initially on a one-year contract with potential renewal, and applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until March 31, 2026.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant
description: This position offers a full-time or part-time research role supporting a project on privacy law and data protection reforms in Hong Kong. The Research Assistant will conduct literature reviews, assist with data collection and stakeholder interviews, analyze research findings, and help prepare outputs and workshop events. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Law or related field, demonstrate strong legal research and writing skills, and be fluent in English and Cantonese with subject-matter interest. The initial contract runs for at least six months with potential renewal based on performance and funding availability.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant role supports a digital humanities project developing generative AI applications for Chinese calligraphy, leveraging a large-scale dataset of historical handwritten documents from the late Qing to early Republican period. Key responsibilities include assisting in the development of two AI models (one for document analysis, one for calligraphy generation), conducting user studies, applying OCR and computer vision techniques to digitize calligraphic materials, managing dataset updates, and coordinating stakeholder communication. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree or higher in a technical discipline (Computer Science, AI, Data Science, or Engineering) with at least two years of post-qualification experience, demonstrating strong expertise in generative AI, computer vision, and machine learning, alongside excellent bilingual communication and the ability to bridge technical and humanities concepts. This position offers distinctive opportunities to work at the intersection of cutting-edge AI research and cultural heritage, collaborating with leading academic teams while contributing to museum exhibitions

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position in pediatric leukemia genomics seeks a researcher with a PhD in genetics, molecular biology, bioinformatics, or related field (obtained within five years) and strong publication record in cancer or functional genomics, along with proficiency in R/Python and genomic analysis pipelines. The fellow will conduct independent and collaborative research on genetic drivers of childhood acute lymphoblastic leukemia, perform large-scale genomic data analysis and molecular biology experiments (including CRISPR-Cas9 editing and ChIP-seq), and prepare manuscripts and grant proposals. The role offers access to advanced genomic platforms and opportunities for multidisciplinary collaboration with national and international partners, with structured mentorship and regular progress reviews. The initial two-year contract is renewable based on performance and funding availability.

title: Research Associate(s) / Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund)
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks Research Associate(s) or Senior Research Assistant(s) to support a large-scale cohort and clinical trial investigating the relationship between sleep, circadian disturbances, and mental health in adolescents through a two-phase study design. Candidates must hold a Master's degree (Research Associate) or Bachelor's degree (Senior Research Assistant) in Social Sciences, Public Health, or related fields, with at least four years of relevant post-qualification experience, alongside proficient English and Cantonese communication skills and strong organisational abilities. Key responsibilities include overseeing project implementation and timelines, recruiting participants through relationships with local schools, supporting junior staff, executing project protocols, conducting data analysis, and report writing. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis, with preference given to candidates with youth mental health research experience and statistical analysis knowledge.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in Finance seeks a recent graduate with strong quantitative and coding skills to support research projects spanning environmental, development, and macroeconomic topics under the supervision of Prof. Henry Zhang and collaborators at Wharton. Essential qualifications include a Bachelor's degree, excellent programming abilities (particularly in R or Python), familiarity with AI tools, fluent English communication, and demonstrated interest in pursuing postgraduate study in Economics or Finance, though formal finance background is not required. Primary responsibilities involve writing R code for data visualization, using APIs to automate PDF data extraction, improving Julia code for equilibrium solvers, and supporting other research tasks as needed. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract, with application materials including CV, transcript, writing sample, code sample, and referee contacts to be submitted through the online portal.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
department: Department of Otorhinolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery
description: This research assistant position supports AI-driven research projects in otolaryngology, focusing on diagnostics, rhinology, skull base surgery, and olfactory sciences. The successful candidate will hold a bachelor's degree in a relevant field with strong competency in data analysis, programming (Python or R), and AI healthcare applications, alongside excellent analytical and communication skills. Primary responsibilities include data collection and analysis, collaborating with clinical teams to implement AI innovations, contributing to publications, and providing administrative and basic laboratory support. The role offers a one-year renewable contract providing hands-on experience in a multidisciplinary research environment.

title: Junior Research Assistant
description: Junior Research Assistant positions are available in Orthopaedics and Traumatology, based at a hospital in Hong Kong. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences, Pharmacology, or related fields, with demonstrated research experience or clinical research interest, and fluency in both Cantonese and English. The role involves supporting team-based research projects, with project management or clinical research experience being advantageous. Appointments are made on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Executive Officer I (Innovation and Impact Development)
description: This role supports a university's knowledge transfer office by leading stakeholder engagement and managing major government and innovation funding programmes such as RAISe+ and TSSSU, including application development and post-award administration. The successful candidate will provide strategic secretarial support to senior committees, prepare impact reports and policy papers, coordinate flagship events and partnerships, and oversee ad-hoc strategic projects. The position requires at least 8 years of relevant executive experience with a proven track record in securing competitive funding, exceptional skills in writing funding applications and strategic documents, bilingual fluency in English and Chinese, and demonstrated ability to manage multiple complex projects independently under tight deadlines. Prior experience in government funding programmes or knowledge transfer within higher education institutions is advantageous; the role is offered on a fixed-term contract of up to 2 years, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate (6-month contract)
department: Office for Greater Bay Area Developments
description: This six-month contract position supports a university office coordinating Greater Bay Area development initiatives, with a focus on organizing a national marine knowledge competition. The Research Associate will manage project execution, coordinate with mainland and Hong Kong stakeholders, assist committees, and provide logistical support for events and daily operations. Candidates must hold a Master's degree with at least four years of relevant experience, demonstrate fluency in English, Cantonese, and Putonghua, and possess strong project coordination skills—preferably with cross-border government collaborations. The role emphasizes interpersonal communication, organizational ability, and proficiency in standard office software.

title: Project Co-ordinator II
department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
description: This Project Co-ordinator II role supports a newly established research centre focused on Electronic Design Automation, requiring comprehensive administrative management across finance, personnel, and research operations. The appointee will handle executive support functions including committee secretarial duties, research project administration, event coordination, and research assistant management. Essential qualifications include a Bachelor's degree, at least two years of post-qualification project management experience (preferably in higher education), bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and strong organisational and interpersonal skills, with conference organisation experience being advantageous. The position is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract.

title: Project Co-ordinator II (Manuscript Editing)
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Press
description: This position involves overseeing the complete publication lifecycle of scholarly manuscripts from submission through final proofing, while coordinating with internal teams and external partners including authors, designers, and indexers. The role requires managing administrative responsibilities such as database records, project budgets, file organization, and report preparation. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Humanities or Social Sciences with at least three years of relevant editorial experience, along with fluent English and Chinese proficiency, strong communication skills, familiarity with the Chicago Manual of Style, and experience editing traditional Chinese materials. The appointment is initially for one year and renewable based on performance.

title: Executive Officer I/II (carrying the job title of ‘Senior Manager / Manager’), Office of Taught Postgraduate Programmes
description: This position manages admissions and student services for taught postgraduate programmes within a leading business school's faculty. The successful candidate will supervise a team handling admissions data, student recruitment events, and stakeholder coordination while reporting to the Administrative Director. Key requirements include a bachelor's degree, at least 8 years of executive experience (preferably in higher education), strong data analytics and presentation skills, and deep knowledge of the Chinese Mainland education sector. The role is distinctive for its requirement to balance operational efficiency with high-quality outputs across a large, internationally diverse student population, and demands flexibility including weekend work and business travel.

title: Assistant Computer Officer
department: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
description: This role involves establishing and maintaining the department's network infrastructure, Linux servers, and IT systems while developing web applications and providing technical support to staff and students. The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field with demonstrated experience in network administration, Linux server management, and web programming, alongside strong problem-solving abilities. Additional expertise in VPN setup, high-performance computing, or Cisco certifications (CCNA/CCNP) would be advantageous. The position is offered initially on a 2-year renewable contract with performance-based continuation.

title: Executive Officer II
description: The Executive Officer II will provide comprehensive executive and administrative support to the Graduate Division and Nethersole School of Nursing, including managing postgraduate programmes, coordinating academic activities, handling student matters, supervising staff, and supporting senior management. Candidates must possess a Bachelor's degree, demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and have at least 5 years of post-qualification executive experience, preferably in academic programme administration at tertiary institutions. Essential competencies include excellent interpersonal and organisational skills, ability to work independently under pressure, strong responsibility and self-motivation, and proficient computer skills, with experience in curriculum management, student affairs, and staff supervision at higher education institutions being advantageous. The position is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract with contract-end gratuity, subject to performance and mutual agreement, and shortlisted candidates will be required to complete a written test.

title: Transport Assistant II(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Assistant Transport Operations Officer’)
description: This position is for an Assistant Transport Operations Officer responsible for monitoring on-campus shuttle services, scheduling bus timetables, arranging vehicle maintenance, managing school bus operations, and handling administrative duties including clerical support, enquiries, and complaints. The successful candidate must have secondary education, professional qualifications in at least five subjects (including English and Chinese), two years of clerical and administrative experience, valid driving licences for private cars and light goods vehicles, strong typing skills, and proficiency in English and Putonghua. Additional requirements include knowledge of vehicle repair and maintenance, computing skills in MS Office, and demonstrated responsibility and teamwork. The role operates five days weekly with potential for outdoor work and overtime, initially offered on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Project Co-ordinator II(s)
department: The Hong Kong Jockey Club Sports Medicine and Health Sciences Centre
description: The position involves coordinating and executing community sports medicine events, providing secretarial and administrative support to sports medicine associations, and assisting with postgraduate programmes and short courses at a hospital-based centre. Candidates require a bachelor's degree and at least three years of experience in event/project management or administrative support, alongside strong problem-solving, interpersonal, and bilingual communication skills in English and Chinese. The role demands self-initiative, the ability to work independently and multitask, and occasional work beyond standard office hours. Appointments are offered initially on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Executive Officer II (carrying the job title of 'Manager, Content and Event'), Alumni Affairs and Development Office
description: This position manages content creation and event execution for a major business school's alumni office, which serves a 47,000-strong global alumni network. The role encompasses full-cycle responsibilities including content production (writing, design, video), digital communications management across CRM and CMS platforms, event planning and logistics, and campaign effectiveness tracking. The appointee will supervise freelance and student contributors, control budgets and timelines, and contribute to alumni engagement strategy development. The ideal candidate holds a bachelor's degree in communication, public relations, advertising, marketing, or related fields, with flexibility for extended hours and business travel required.

title: Clerical Assistant(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Operations Assistant’)
description: This Operations Assistant position in the Transport Office combines clerical administrative work with customer-facing and operational duties. Responsibilities include standard office tasks (filing, data entry), counter service with passenger enquiry and complaint handling, management of campus school bus services and facilities, and occasional driving duties as needed. Candidates must have completed secondary education, strong word processing and computer skills, a valid driving licence for cars and light goods vehicles, fluent written and spoken English and Chinese, and excellent interpersonal and customer service abilities. The role is offered on a two-year renewable contract, with preference given to applicants with prior experience in customer service or transportation sectors.

title: Executive Officer II (carrying the job title of 'Manager, CRM and Digital Engagement'), Alumni Affairs and Development Office
description: This role manages the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems and digital platforms for an alumni affairs office, supporting a network of over 47,000 alumni across the business school. Key responsibilities include overseeing CRM tools and data management (data entry, cleansing, segmentation, and audits), generating engagement reports, coordinating events and stakeholder relations, and implementing data governance protocols. The successful candidate will need strong technical proficiency with CRM platforms and systems, excellent project management and communication skills, and the ability to work collaboratively across internal teams and external vendors. The position requires flexibility regarding hours and travel, with opportunities to shape alumni engagement strategy through data-driven insights.

title: Assistant Librarian II (carrying the job title of 'Special Collections Librarian')
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Library
description: This position seeks a Special Collections Librarian to manage and develop a significant collection of Chinese and Hong Kong studies materials, ranging from ancient oracle bones to contemporary literary archives. The role involves acquisition and donor relations, faculty liaison, preservation and cataloging using standard archival systems, exhibition development, and outreach activities, with some shift work required at library helpdesks. Candidates must hold a postgraduate qualification in librarianship or information science with at least three years of professional experience in special collections, demonstrating expertise in collection management, archival standards (DACS, EAD, MARC), archives management systems, and collaborative skills. The position is a two-year renewable contract, with preference given to applicants possessing advanced degrees in China Studies or related disciplines and proficiency in both Chinese and English.

title: Assistant Librarian II (carrying the job title of ‘Digital Transformation Services Librarian’)
department: The Chinese University of Hong Kong Library
description: This position leads digital transformation initiatives within the library, reporting to the Head of Digital Initiatives. The role encompasses developing and implementing cutting-edge digital technologies, managing the full lifecycle of web and software applications, overseeing digitization and digital repository services, and driving organizational change to support data-driven research. The successful candidate must hold a university degree in computer science or software engineering combined with a postgraduate qualification in librarianship or information science, along with at least three years of professional-level experience in digital development; technical competencies required include proficiency in Python, web application development, familiarity with emerging technologies such as text-mining and computer vision, and demonstrated expertise in agile methodologies and change management. The role includes shift work at library helpdesks during evenings, weekends, and holidays.

title: Project Co-ordinator II(s) (carrying the job title of ‘Executive, Corporate Communications’)
description: The Communications and Public Relations Office seeks Project Co-ordinators II to support the university's strategic communications objectives across media relations, event management, and content creation. Key responsibilities include planning and executing press events, managing media enquiries and relationships, producing bilingual publicity materials, and addressing sensitive communications issues. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree (preferably in Communications or Journalism), have at least 5 years of experience in public relations or corporate communications, and demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua) alongside strong organisational and interpersonal skills. The position is offered on an initial 2-year renewable contract and requires candidates to pass a written test in both English and Chinese.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (The Hong Kong Genome Project)
description: This position supports a government-backed genomic medicine initiative, requiring a Bachelor's degree in genetics, health sciences, biomedical science, or related fields, with at least two years of relevant experience. The role involves conducting basic research, managing project correspondence, assisting genetic counsellors, recruiting and interviewing study participants in clinical settings, and handling administrative duties. Candidates should be detail-oriented team players with strong English and Chinese language skills, though experience in patient care, clinical research, or digital health technologies is advantageous. The appointment is initially contract-based for one year, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate (carrying the job title of “Project Manager”)
description: The Department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology seeks a Research Associate/Project Manager to establish and manage high-quality prospective clinical studies. Candidates must hold a Master's degree in Biology, Biochemistry, Life Science or related field, with at least four years of clinical research experience, fluency in English and Chinese (Cantonese and Putonghua), and strong interpersonal skills. Key responsibilities include developing management systems, securing regulatory approvals, coordinating study documentation, training research sites, managing budgets, monitoring protocol compliance, supervising data collection and validation, and reporting progress to oversight committees and funders. The position is offered initially on a one-year fixed contract, renewable based on performance.

title: Assistant Director of Human Resources (at the rank of Senior Assistant Secretary II)
description: The position is an Assistant Director of Human Resources responsible for overseeing recruitment, staff engagement, performance management, benefits, and learning and development while developing HR policies and procedures across the institution. The role emphasizes leading talent development strategies, including performance management enhancement, leadership development, and succession planning, requiring the appointee to lead diverse teams and partner with academic and administrative units on workforce planning and regulatory compliance. Candidates must hold a good honours degree with strong English and Chinese language proficiency (including Putonghua), possess substantial senior-level HR or public administration experience preferably in higher education, and demonstrate expertise in employment law, strategic communication, analytical ability, and committee work. The appointment is offered on a three-year renewable contract basis, with shortlisted candidates required to complete a written test.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on lung cancer research, including genomics, tumor microenvironment investigation, and therapeutic development. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Biological or Chemical Sciences with molecular biology experience, or a Ph.D. in Computer Science with bioinformatics expertise, along with demonstrated commitment to research. The fellow will conduct independent research, publish findings, apply for grants, and mentor junior researchers at a hospital research facility. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time research assistant position requires a Bachelor's degree in a health-related field (preferably Nursing) plus two to three years of relevant work experience, with demonstrated capability in conducting and coordinating research studies using both qualitative and quantitative methods. Key responsibilities include assisting with data collection, implementing intervention protocols, performing literature reviews, managing data, and writing reports, while maintaining proficiency in English and Chinese and competence with MS Office and SPSS. The role demands independent work ability and offers a one-year renewable contract commencing immediately, with candidates required to submit their CV, qualification certificates, and supporting documents through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position supports breast cancer research, requiring either a PhD or a Master's degree with five years of cancer research experience. The successful candidate will conduct experimental work in cell biology, animal studies, and molecular techniques while collaborating with postgraduate students and supporting the writing of manuscripts and grant proposals at a hospital-based research facility. Key competencies include solid technical expertise in molecular biology, strong research motivation, and data management skills, with next-generation sequencing and bioinformatics experience being advantageous. The initial appointment is for one year on a contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
department: Department of Anatomical and Cellular Pathology
description: This position is a Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant in pathology conducting cancer research across multiple sites. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Biomedical Sciences or related fields, with practical experience in animal handling, tissue culture, and molecular analytical techniques. The role involves collaborative research with department staff and offers an initial one-year contract renewable by mutual agreement, making it suitable for recent graduates seeking postgraduate experience. Required application materials include a CV detailing academic qualifications and research experience, academic certificates, and two reference letters.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (carrying the job title of ‘Project Officer/Assistant Project Officer’)
description: This Research Assistant position in Electronic Engineering requires a Bachelor's degree and preferably two years of tertiary experience, with strong bilingual English-Chinese communication skills and proficiency in MS Office and graphic design. The role involves assisting with program administration, project management, and supporting the organization of postgraduate events and activities. Applicants must demonstrate responsibility, self-motivation, and independent working capability; fresh graduates are eligible. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract, with applications submitted online via cover letter and CV.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks Research Assistant(s) or Junior Research Assistant(s) to support a large-scale study investigating the relationship between sleep, circadian disturbances, and mental health in adolescents through a two-phase cohort and clinical trial design. Key responsibilities include conducting clinical and research interviews, performing neurocognitive and sleep assessments, assisting with psychological intervention development, and supporting data entry and analysis. Ideal candidates hold a Bachelor's degree or above in social sciences, public health, or related fields, demonstrate proficiency in English and Cantonese, and possess strong organizational and research skills; prior experience in youth mental health or statistical analysis is advantageous. Positions are offered on a one-year renewable contract basis with on-the-job training provided.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) – Cross Cultural Analytics (Data Science, NLP, Computational Linguistics, Computational Humanities)
description: This postdoctoral position invites two fellows with PhDs in computer science, NLP, computational linguistics, or related technical fields to join an interdisciplinary project applying data science and AI methods to study Chinese cultural influence in Europe and North America from 1550–1900. Applicants must have strong Python programming skills, experience with modern NLP tools and textual data processing (including OCR, embeddings, and transformers), and the ability to work independently in interdisciplinary teams; multilingual NLP experience and knowledge of digital text pipelines, graph neural networks, or semantic search are preferred. The fellows will develop and apply NLP methods for historical text analysis, construct multilingual corpora, co-author research papers for digital humanities and NLP venues, and collaborate with an international research team across China, Europe, and North America. The appointment is initially for eighteen months from June 2026, renewable based on performance, with applications reviewed from 15

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Orthopaedics and Traumatology invites applications for Postdoctoral Fellow(s) and Research Assistant(s) to support AI-enabled translational research projects integrating artificial intelligence, augmented reality, and surgical navigation technologies. Postdoctoral Fellows should hold a PhD in Biomedical Engineering, Computer Science, or related fields with proven expertise in grant writing, publications, and project management, while Research Assistants require a Bachelor's or Master's degree and strong interest in medical technologies and AI. Key responsibilities include preparing grant proposals and manuscripts, coordinating collaborations, assisting with software development and clinical data management, and tracking project progress. Positions are offered on a one-year renewable contract basis, with preference given to candidates experienced in AI/ML, medical imaging, or surgical technology.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: The School of Journalism and Communication seeks a Postdoctoral Fellow or Research Associate to support research on corporate social responsibility and sustainability communication, requiring a PhD or Master's degree in Communication or related fields (Business, Marketing, Media Studies, Psychology, or Organisational Studies) with at least four years of relevant experience. The successful candidate will demonstrate expertise in both quantitative and qualitative research methods, with preference for computational or data-driven approaches, and will manage research protocols, data analysis, publication coordination, and research activity administration across interdisciplinary projects. Key responsibilities include developing research methodologies, supporting data collection and interpretation, and disseminating findings through academic channels. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance and funding availability, and requires strong English communication skills and the ability to work on stakeholder-oriented research questions.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: The Department of Surgery seeks a Research Assistant to support machine learning research focused on improving diagnosis and clinical workflows for thoracic aortic conditions. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree or above in healthcare, biomedical engineering, computer science, or a related field, with foundational knowledge of cardiovascular anatomy, medical imaging, and demonstrated expertise in developing machine learning systems. Primary responsibilities include designing and optimizing machine learning algorithms for standardized diagnosis and reporting, alongside general research administration duties. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance, and requires fluency in English and strong organizational and communication skills.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the Department of Finance seeks candidates with a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or related fields, along with strong programming proficiency in R, MATLAB, and/or Python. The role involves assisting with actuarial science research and performing assigned duties, with preference given to applicants with prior research experience in actuarial science or statistics. The position is initially offered on a six-month renewable contract, contingent on performance and mutual agreement, and requires applicants to demonstrate their programming capabilities and research background in their application materials.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in nursing requires a Bachelor's degree plus at least two years of relevant experience in nursing, public health, or related disciplines, with demonstrated expertise in quantitative and qualitative research, grant writing, and manuscript preparation. Key responsibilities include initiating and conducting research projects, analyzing data, drafting grant proposals and academic publications, and supporting the School Research Committee administratively. Proficiency in both English and Chinese (written and spoken) is essential. The role is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis and requires submission of a CV and academic certificates demonstrating language proficiency and qualifications.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant (carrying the job title of ‘Project Officer/Assistant Project Officer’)
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: This position offers a research support role within a mechanical engineering department, carrying the title of Project Officer or Assistant Project Officer depending on experience level. Applicants require a bachelor's degree and preferably 2-3 years of tertiary institution experience, along with strong bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, excellent communication and organizational skills, and proficiency with standard office software and Chinese word processing. The role involves assisting with office administration, project management, and coordinating postgraduate programme events and activities. The appointment is offered on a two-year renewable contract basis, with prior postgraduate programme administration experience valued but not essential.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This role involves supporting sleep and mental health research studies through participant recruitment, conducting clinical and research interviews, data entry and analysis, and other assigned tasks. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Psychology, Counselling, Medical Sciences, Social Work, or related fields, be proficient Cantonese speakers with strong communication skills, and demonstrate organization, reliability, and enthusiasm for research and clinical translation in mental and sleep health. Prior experience in psychological or psychiatric research is preferred, and the position requires occasional work outside standard hours and weekends. The appointment is initially a three-month contract, renewable by mutual agreement, with on-the-job training provided.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Statistics and Data Science
description: This Research Assistant position is embedded within the Racing Laboratory at a major racecourse, where the appointee will work on discovering and validating biomarkers for equine doping control through proteomic and transcriptomic analysis. The role requires a Bachelor's degree in Molecular Biology, Biochemistry, or related field, with strong laboratory skills in PCR, next-generation sequencing, and/or mass spectrometry, alongside fluency in English and Chinese and excellent data organization abilities. Key responsibilities include analyzing specimens for biomarkers and prohibited substances, performing routine instrument maintenance, and ensuring compliance with ISO/IEC 17025:2017 quality standards while collaborating with a multidisciplinary team of biochemists, analysts, and statisticians. This distinctive opportunity uniquely combines employment by a major research university with practical work in a world-leading equine testing facility.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Postdoctoral fellows are sought to conduct nursing and health research, developing new projects, managing ongoing studies, and contributing to grant writing and academic publications. Candidates must hold a PhD in nursing, public health, health-related fields, or social sciences, with demonstrated expertise in quantitative or qualitative research, strong publication records, and fluency in both English and Chinese.
