The position offers an initial one-year contract, renewable based on performance and agreement. Applicants should apply online through the designated university careers portal.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The position involves supporting research projects in molecular diagnostics and circulating DNA at two facilities, with responsibilities including assisting with molecular testing, data analysis, sample processing, and routine laboratory operations using techniques such as PCR and DNA sequencing. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Medical Laboratory Science, Biomedical Science, Life Science, or related fields, with preferred laboratory experience and fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese (Cantonese and Mandarin). The role requires self-motivation, responsibility, and strong teamwork abilities. The appointment is initially for one year on a contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position is based at a hospital facility and supports public health research focused on behavioral and mental health promotion across Hong Kong and mainland China. The role involves coordinating research activities, leading group-based health promotion projects, and conducting data analysis, with on-the-job training provided in research methodology, biostatistics, and epidemiology. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in public health, biostatistics, epidemiology, psychology, or related fields, with statistical skills and fluency in English and Cantonese; prior research coordination or intervention project experience is advantageous. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Sports Medicine Team seeks Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, or Research Assistants to conduct basic research on musculoskeletal tissue repair within an orthopaedics department. Candidates should hold a PhD in Bioinformatics, Biomedical Sciences, Bioengineering, or Biology with strong molecular and cell biology skills (cell culture, RNA-seq, qPCR, flow cytometry), a solid publication record, and ability to work independently; those with MPhil or BSc degrees plus relevant experience may also be considered for lower-level positions. Key responsibilities include studying therapeutic effects on tissue repair, identifying novel markers and pathways of tendinopathy through transcriptomics and lipidomics, preparing grants and publications, and managing laboratory duties. The role offers a one-year renewable contract with the possibility for appointees to pursue their own PhD research concurrently.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports quantitative marketing research projects led by a faculty member. The role involves organizing data collection, conducting literature reviews, managing research data, and providing administrative support, requiring a Bachelor's or Master's degree in economics, statistics, or related fields, along with strong programming skills in Excel, R, and/or Python, excellent English proficiency, and demonstrated ability to execute detailed work methodically and reliably. The position is contract-based for six months, renewable based on performance, and prior experience as a Research Assistant in quantitative research is preferred. Applicants must submit a CV with academic references and a cover letter detailing relevant research experience through the university's online application portal.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the Department of Surgery requires a Master's degree in Biochemistry, Biological Sciences, or a related field, along with demonstrated expertise in mammalian cell culture and molecular techniques such as DNA/RNA extraction, RT-PCR, cytotoxicity assays, and immunofluorescence imaging. The role involves working directly with clinical specimens, including medical sample collection and processing of patient-derived organoids, making it distinctive for its clinical research focus. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement, commencing as soon as possible.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: This position offers research opportunities at three levels within a project developing integrated unmanned aerial vehicle systems and a digital traffic platform. Postdoctoral Fellows and Research Associates will conduct advanced research in UAV optimization (control, navigation, and task planning) and traffic prediction algorithms, while Research Assistants will provide supporting roles including data collection, labeling, field testing, and model training. Candidates must hold a PhD in relevant engineering or computer science disciplines for postdoctoral roles, a Master's degree with four years of experience for associate positions, or a Bachelor's degree for assistant roles. Appointments are contract-based for up to two years beginning March 2026, with renewal contingent on performance, and applicants must submit detailed CVs and academic certificates through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s) (External Fund)
department: The Jockey Club School of Public Health and Primary Care
description: The school is recruiting Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants to conduct health research using large administrative datasets. Candidates must hold a PhD in public health, epidemiology, biostatistics, or related fields (for postdoctoral roles), or a Master's degree with relevant experience, and demonstrate proficiency in statistical software and quantitative analysis. Responsibilities include developing research projects, managing datasets, writing proposals and publications, and assisting the Principal Investigator. Positions are initially contracted for one year with the possibility of renewal based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: Applicants with a Master's degree (Research Assistant) or Bachelor's/Diploma (Junior Research Assistant) in biological or medical sciences are sought to support microbiology research. Primary responsibilities include managing bacterial strains and stocks, ordering laboratory supplies, conducting laboratory tests, and performing experiments with associated data collection and reporting. The role offers hands-on experience in microbiology, biochemistry, and molecular biology, with work split between a hospital setting and a science park. The initial one-year contract position is renewable based on mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: The Jockey Club School of Public Health and Primary Care
description: This role offers Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant positions requiring a Master's degree (or Bachelor's for junior level) in Statistics, Public Health, Social Science, or related fields, with proficiency in statistical software (SPSS, STATA, SAS, R) and/or qualitative analysis tools (NVIVO). Key responsibilities include conducting literature reviews, data extraction and quality assessments, performing quantitative and qualitative research analyses, managing datasets, and preparing project reports, with strong organizational, communication, and bilingual English-Chinese skills (Cantonese fluency preferred) essential. The position is initially contract-based for up to six months, renewable based on performance, with preference given to candidates with experience in evidence-based medicine, guideline development, or traditional Chinese medicine backgrounds.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral fellowship in finance requires a Ph.D. in Actuarial Science or related field, with demonstrated research excellence, strong English proficiency, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. The fellow will conduct high-quality actuarial research, develop research proposals, write articles and reports, and present findings under faculty supervision. The one-year contract position begins in January 2026 and requires submission of a cover letter, CV with complete publication list, research proposal, two recent publications, and three referee contacts through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks Research Assistant(s) to support clinical and translational research projects across diverse ophthalmic conditions including glaucoma, retinal diseases, ocular cancers, and dry eye disease. Key responsibilities include conducting ophthalmic investigations, extracting medical data, and coordinating patient follow-up schedules, with opportunities to develop specialized diagnostic skills and contribute to landmark publications. Applicants require a Bachelor's degree, proficiency in English and Cantonese, research interest in ophthalmology, and patient-focused interpersonal skills; experience with statistical analysis and clinical research is advantageous, though fresh graduates are welcome to apply. Initial appointments are contract-based for one year with renewal potential, and successful candidates may pursue postgraduate degrees supported by the department.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: School of Hotel and Tourism Management
description: The School of Hotel and Tourism Management seeks Postdoctoral Fellows to conduct independent and collaborative research in real estate economics, urban economics, environmental economics, and related fields. Candidates must hold a PhD (or be ABD) in economics, finance, real estate, public policy, management, or related disciplines, with strong programming skills in statistical software and solid knowledge of econometrics and mathematical modeling. Fellows will be responsible for producing high-quality research suitable for top-tier publication and contributing to the school's intellectual and research culture, working alongside faculty experts in real estate finance, household finance, and urban economics. The appointment is initially for one year on a contract basis, commencing as soon as possible or in August 2026, with the possibility of renewal by mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Department of Geography and Resource Management
description: This postdoctoral position involves conducting original, high-quality research under the supervision of Prof. Jack Harris in areas such as evolutionary economic geography, sustainable transitions, Green FinTech, and entrepreneurial ecosystems. Candidates must hold a PhD in Human Geography or related disciplines, demonstrate a strong publication record, possess excellent English proficiency, and show capability for both independent and collaborative work. The role is offered on an 18-month contract basis, with the start date flexible until March 1st, 2026. Applicants should submit a cover letter, CV, two-page research plan, and three referee contacts through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position in a pharmacy research team involves leading a Chinese medicine-funded project with responsibilities spanning research logistics, fieldwork oversight, data management, and mentoring of junior staff. Essential qualifications include a PhD in a healthcare or scientific discipline, strong statistical software proficiency, fluency in Cantonese and English, and proven independent research capability, with preference given to candidates with experience in artificial intelligence, project management, or implementation science. The role requires coordinating multi-site studies, developing educational materials, ensuring data quality across collection through analysis, and organizing training sessions. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate
description: This Research Associate position supports a pharmaceutical research project within a pharmacy school, requiring a doctoral degree in applied mathematics, engineering, statistics, pharmaceutical sciences, or related life sciences. The role demands strong programming skills (Python, PHP, JavaScript, Java, or similar), with beneficial experience in pharmacology, genetics, pharmacometrics, or systems pharmacology, alongside excellent communication abilities and proven capacity for both independent and collaborative work. Key responsibilities include acquiring pharmaceutical data via web crawlers, classifying and extracting information using deep learning or large language models, developing software interfaces, and monitoring project progress. The position is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position supports a Chinese Medicine Development Fund project within a pharmacy school, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Pharmacy or related field and strong proficiency in English-language scientific literature, data management, and office productivity tools. The role involves systematically searching, collecting, organizing, and synthesizing pharmaceutical materials and databases; extracting and standardizing key data points; and maintaining accurate records to support the research team's work. The successful candidate must demonstrate meticulous attention to detail, analytical skills, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively under tight deadlines. The position is offered initially on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position in nursing requires either a PhD in nursing or a health-related field, or a Master's degree with four years of post-qualification research experience. The role focuses on initiating and developing research projects, conducting empirical studies, analyzing data, and supporting the preparation of grant proposals and academic publications. Candidates must demonstrate strong quantitative and/or qualitative research capabilities with an established publication record, along with fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese including Cantonese. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Finance seeks a Research Associate or Research Assistant to support its research operations and the Finance for Society Research Centre, which connects academic researchers with industry practitioners on topics including quantitative trading, sustainable finance, behavioral finance, and fintech. The appointee will provide research support, assist with departmental and centre activities, handle administrative tasks, and coordinate with internal and external stakeholders, reporting to the Department Chairperson. Candidates must hold a PhD in Finance, Economics, or Business (for Research Associate) or a Master's degree in these fields (for Research Assistant), with strong data analysis and policy research skills, fluency in English and Chinese (including Cantonese and Putonghua), and demonstrated ability to work independently and collaboratively. The position is initially offered on a two-year contract, renewable based on performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral or research associate position supports a major centre for Chinese music research with extensive archival collections spanning books, recordings, and primary sources on Chinese opera. The role combines research administration, grant writing, donor engagement, and event management, with responsibilities including funding applications, public engagement, database development, and coordination of research activities across the department. Candidates must hold a PhD in Music or related fields (or a Master's with four years' relevant experience), demonstrate fluency in English and Chinese, and possess expertise in grant applications, conference organization, archival documentation, and media promotion. The position is offered on a two-year renewable contract and requires both independent and collaborative working within the department's faculty structure.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The position supports preclinical PET/MR imaging research at a newly established neuroscience research institute, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Medical Imaging, Radiography, or a related field. Responsibilities include daily facility operations such as equipment maintenance and calibration, coordinating compliance with campus and animal care services, managing facility bookings and procurement, and ensuring ethical standards for animal research. The ideal candidate should have strong organizational and communication skills, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, familiarity with MRI technology, and preferably prior experience in laboratory research and animal handling. The role is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable subject to satisfactory performance.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Mechanical and Automation Engineering
description: This research assistant position focuses on developing learning-based autonomous robotic systems for surgical procedures, combining simulation and physical hardware work with emphasis on multi-arm coordination and embodied intelligence. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Robotics, or Engineering, with demonstrated knowledge of imitation and reinforcement learning, hands-on experience with robotics simulators, and proficiency in deep learning frameworks like PyTorch. Key responsibilities include building surgical simulation platforms using Isaac-SIM, implementing state-of-the-art learning algorithms for surgical tasks, transferring trained policies to physical robots, and supporting real-world experiments with robotic hardware. The role is distinctive for its intersection of AI, robotics, and medical applications, with preferred qualifications in Isaac Sim, ROS/ROS2, simulation expertise, and prior robotic hardware experience.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant
description: The position is a full-time or part-time research assistant focused on supporting research administration in preventive medicine and healthy lifestyle promotion. Key responsibilities include providing administrative support for research projects, assisting with grant applications, organizing research activities, and liaising between academic departments and external partners. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree in IT, informatics, health, psychology, or related fields, with at least two years of research-related experience, plus strong communication skills in English and Chinese, technical competency, and the ability to work independently. The role is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: Research assistants are sought to support health-risk behaviour research projects, requiring a bachelor's degree or higher in disciplines such as statistics, psychology, social sciences, or public health, along with fluency in English, Chinese, and Cantonese, strong computer literacy, and excellent organisational and communication skills. Primary responsibilities include implementing interventions, recruiting participants and collecting data, conducting literature reviews, managing data entry, and preparing reports and publications. Candidates must demonstrate self-motivation, responsibility, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. Positions are offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks Research Assistants or Junior Research Assistants to support clinical and research programmes across academic, community, and translational initiatives, focusing on diverse eye diseases including glaucoma, retinal conditions, and ocular cancers. Key responsibilities include conducting ophthalmic investigations, managing medical data, and coordinating participant follow-up schedules. Applicants require a Bachelor's degree (or equivalent experience for Junior positions), proficiency in English and Cantonese, strong organizational skills, and genuine interest in ophthalmology research; prior research experience and statistical analysis skills are advantageous. The role offers opportunities to contribute to landmark research projects, develop specialized ophthalmic investigative skills, and pursue career advancement including postgraduate studies, with initial appointment on a one-year renewable contract.

title: Research Assistant
department: Institute for Tissue Engineering and Regenerative Medicine
description: This Research Assistant position supports tissue engineering and regenerative medicine research, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences or related field (Master's preferred) and practical expertise in primary cell culture, histology, immunohistochemistry, and data analysis. The role involves conducting research under supervisor guidance, assisting laboratory personnel with technical training, and performing assigned laboratory duties, with on-the-job training provided to develop specialized skills. Candidates must be self-motivated, well-organized, independent workers with strong communication abilities in English and Chinese. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral position in the School of Biomedical Sciences seeks a PhD-holder with expertise in stem cell engineering, sequencing/gene editing technology, or regenerative biology for nerve injury research. The successful candidate will support the Principal Investigator in project management, establish and maintain research platforms, supervise junior team members, and contribute to manuscript and grant preparation. Applicants must demonstrate strong publication records, bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively under pressure. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: This Research Assistant position in Orthopaedics and Traumatology focuses on advancing AI technology and optimizing large language models for medical applications using Python, R, PyTorch, and TensorFlow. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Biomedical Science, Computer Science, or related fields, with demonstrated expertise in unsupervised deep learning, statistical programming, and proficiency in both English and Chinese. Key responsibilities include conducting clinical trials, analyzing data, manipulating medical images, and implementing deep learning solutions, with additional requirements for JavaScript and Vue.js framework familiarity. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract starting immediately.

title: Part-time Research Assistant
description: This part-time Research Assistant position in the School of Pharmacy supports Prof. Joyce You's research team, primarily conducting health economics research involving retrospective data collection and analysis of medical costs, literature reviews, and decision-analytic modeling. Candidates must hold at least a Bachelor's degree in Health Economics or related fields (Master's preferred) and demonstrate bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English alongside strong communication skills. The role encompasses database management, Delphi surveys, and various research support tasks assigned by the principal investigator. Applications require submission of a CV and relevant certificates through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position seeks a Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant in the School of Biomedical Sciences to conduct research on nerve injury and repair projects while coordinating laboratory operations. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Medical or Biomedical Sciences and demonstrate at least three years of technical experience in animal studies, particularly microsurgical techniques on rodent, rabbit, or pig models. Additional qualifications include proficiency in tissue sectioning, immunohistochemistry, neuro-electrophysiology, and related techniques, along with strong self-motivation and responsibility. The role is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract contingent on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: Department of Geography and Resource Management
description: Full-time postdoctoral positions are available to conduct research on urban land use change modelling, climate change adaptation, smart cities, or urban informatics in China and the Greater Bay Area. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Human Geography, Urban Studies, GIS, or related fields, with demonstrated expertise in spatial data analysis, GIS, R, Python, and statistical software, along with strong English proficiency and a track record of quality publications. The role involves conducting independent research, preparing proposals and articles, and presenting findings, with preferred experience in cellular automata modelling, agent-based modelling, or GeoAI. The initial appointment is for one year commencing July 2025, renewable based on performance.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: A Research Associate or Research Assistant position is available in a biomedical sciences research group focusing on cellular studies and functional characterization of disease-causing genes. Applicants must hold a Master's degree or above in biomedical research or a related field; Research Associates additionally require at least four years of post-qualification experience. Experience in stem cell culture and CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing is particularly valued. The role is offered on an initial one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance, and candidates should submit a curriculum vitae and reference letter through the online application portal.

title: Research Assistant
description: This Research Assistant position in the School of Life Sciences involves participating in a butterfly-related research project, with responsibilities including conducting field and molecular biology experiments, drafting reports, collaborating with team members, and providing administrative support. Applicants require a Bachelor's degree or higher in Biology or a related field, bilingual proficiency in Chinese and English, and demonstrated self-motivation and organizational skills, though laboratory experience is advantageous and fresh graduates are encouraged to apply. The role is initially contracted for one year with potential renewal, and on-the-job training will be provided. Applications must be submitted online through the institutional career portal.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks full-time or part-time research assistants to support clinical and research programs focusing on ophthalmology and visual sciences, including glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy, and other eye diseases, with particular emphasis on Chinese medicine treatments for dry eye disease. Candidates should hold a Bachelor's degree in Chinese Medicine, Medicine, Public Health, Statistics, or related life sciences, demonstrate proficiency in English and Cantonese, and possess strong organisational and analytical skills; preference is given to registered Chinese Medicine Practitioners with relevant clinical or laboratory experience. Responsibilities include assisting with clinical studies (literature reviews, data collection and analysis), laboratory work (specimen handling), and other assigned duties, with flexibility for shift work including evenings and weekends. Initial appointments are contract-based for one to two years, renewable based on mutual agreement and funding availability.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)
description: The position offers postdoctoral or research associate opportunities within a multidisciplinary neuroscience team investigating RNA regulation of brain circuit formation, maintenance, and plasticity using advanced techniques such as 2-photon optogenetics, imaging, and whole-cell electrophysiology. Candidates must hold a PhD in life sciences with strong publication records, demonstrated expertise in electrophysiology and/or advanced imaging, and excellent communication skills, with additional proficiency in molecular biology, bioinformatics, or microscopy optics being advantageous. Core responsibilities include conducting experiments and analysis, presenting findings at conferences, manuscript writing, assisting with project reports, mentoring team members, and other assigned tasks. The laboratory emphasizes supportive mentorship and career development within a vibrant international research environment at a top-ranked institution.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: The position involves supporting a neuroscience research laboratory investigating RNA regulation of brain circuit formation and plasticity using advanced techniques including 2-photon imaging, optogenetics, and electrophysiology. Responsibilities encompass routine laboratory management such as preparing solutions, maintaining animal colonies, ordering reagents, and equipment maintenance, alongside administrative tasks including budget management and research documentation. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in life sciences with prior research experience, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, and strong organizational and teamwork skills, with expertise in molecular biology, neuroscience, electrophysiology, or microscopy being advantageous. The role offers mentorship and career development opportunities within a collaborative research environment.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: An interdisciplinary research team is recruiting Postdoctoral Fellows and Research Assistants to advance a smart window project focused on developing durable, scalable technology. Candidates must hold a doctoral degree in Chemical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Materials Engineering, or related fields, with a strong research track record, independent work capability, and excellent communication skills; prior experience in smart materials development, light/heat management, or industrial applications is preferred. Appointees will contribute to R&D and commercialization efforts, including smart window design and development, structural optimization, process development, prototype fabrication, performance testing, and collaborative laboratory work. Positions are offered initially on a one-year renewable contract basis contingent on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant / Junior Research Assistant
description: This position is for a Research Assistant or Junior Research Assistant in a genomics center focused on plant-environment interactions and sustainable agriculture. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree in Chemistry, Molecular Biology, Plant Biology, or related field, with strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to work both independently and collaboratively; laboratory experience is advantageous. The role involves conducting molecular experiments and analytical analyses, designing and executing research projects, managing plant materials, collecting and interpreting experimental data, and maintaining detailed records. Applicants should submit a curriculum vitae and academic certificates through the online application portal.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Assistant
department: Institute for Tissue Engineering and Regenerative Medicine
description: The laboratory seeks a Research Assistant or Postdoctoral Fellow to contribute to tissue engineering research focused on tendons and bones, with specific emphasis on extracellular matrix-based biomaterials, stem cell therapies, and exosome-based treatments. Research Assistants require a Bachelor's degree in biomedical science or engineering and strong organizational and communication skills, while Postdoctoral Fellows must hold a doctoral degree and demonstrate independent research capability. Both positions involve conducting experiments, managing laboratory operations, and training staff members. Appointments are initially contract-based for one year, renewable by mutual agreement, with applicants required to submit a comprehensive CV, academic certificates, and referee contacts.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: The Department of Biomedical Engineering is recruiting Postdoctoral Fellow(s) to conduct interdisciplinary research on brain structure and function, involving neuroimaging data analysis and computational model development under the supervision of a senior researcher. Candidates must hold a PhD in engineering, computer science, mathematics, or related fields, with strong quantitative skills in mathematics and programming (MATLAB, Python), and prior experience with neuroimaging (MRI, fMRI, EEG) or machine learning is highly valued. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance, and applicants should submit a CV and statement of interest demonstrating how their expertise aligns with the role's requirements.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associates(s)
description: The Department of Electronic Engineering seeks Postdoctoral Fellow(s) or Research Associate(s) to work on deep learning and remote sensing projects, specifically developing high-definition maps of Hong Kong using LiDAR, imagery, and GNSS data. Candidates must hold a PhD in Computer Science, Electronic Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related fields, with demonstrated experience in research project development, manuscript preparation, data collection and analysis, and fluent English communication. The role involves initiating and conducting research, managing data workflows, contributing to publications and reports, and supporting the Principal Investigator as needed. The initial one-year contract position is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: These research positions in electronic engineering seek candidates with a Bachelor's degree to assist with developing deep learning models, managing projects, and collecting/analyzing data within the department. Ideal applicants will demonstrate strong responsibility and attention to detail, analytical and interpersonal skills, bilingual proficiency in English and Chinese, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. Key responsibilities include training deep learning models, writing research reports, coordinating with collaborating parties, and performing additional assigned duties. The role is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract, with relevant work experience considered advantageous.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund) / Research Assistant(s)
department: Department of Sports Science and Physical Education
description: The Department of Sports Science and Physical Education seeks Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, Senior Research Assistants, or Research Assistants (depending on qualifications) to support externally-funded research projects. Candidates must hold a PhD, Master's, or Bachelor's degree in Physical Education, Sports Science, Exercise Science, Rehabilitation, Public Health, Social Work, or related fields, with progressively increasing experience requirements by level, alongside fluent English and Chinese proficiency and strong interpersonal skills. Key responsibilities include liaising with schools, NGOs, and vendors; planning and delivering project activities; conducting impact assessments; analysing data and generating reports; and supervising junior staff and students. Experience working with persons with disabilities or special needs in educational or community settings is advantageous, and initial appointments are on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)/ Research Assistant(s)
description: Positions are available for Postdoctoral Fellows, Research Associates, and Research Assistants within the Department of Information Engineering's advanced wireless networks laboratory, focusing on wireless networks, stochastic control, and AI/ML applications. Postdoctoral and Associate-level candidates must hold a PhD in a relevant discipline with a demonstrated research track record, while Assistant-level candidates require a Bachelor's degree with strong academic performance and related research experience; hands-on experience with 5G/WiFi systems is advantageous. Appointees will conduct research in the specified areas, with senior-level staff also supervising graduate and undergraduate students. Initial contracts are for one year and renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Associate / Research Assistant
description: The Department of Psychiatry seeks a Research Associate or Research Assistant to support Computational Neuroscience projects translating systems neuroscience insights into clinical applications, with a focus on brain-wide neural datasets from an international collaborative network studying decision-making and circuit-based neuropathological conditions. Candidates must hold a Master's degree (or Bachelor's for the Assistant role) in Computational Neuroscience or quantitative disciplines, with strong programming skills for analyzing neural and behavioral data, proficiency in English, and demonstrated experience in statistics, Python, or machine learning. Primary responsibilities include analyzing and modeling biological data, developing analytical methodologies, disseminating findings, and collaborating across research groups while engaging with scientific and clinical communities. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract basis with opportunities for on-the-job training, international exposure, and publication, with potential for renewal and career development.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The Gerald Choa Neuroscience Institute seeks postdoctoral fellows, research associates, or research assistants to conduct auditory neuroscience research, including studies on auditory perception, processing, pathology, and cochlear implants. Candidates must hold a PhD in neuroscience or related fields, demonstrate proficiency in Python or MATLAB, possess strong data analysis and signal processing skills, and be willing to work with both human participants and animal models. Key responsibilities include conducting experiments, supporting research design and analysis, collaborating with team members, and mentoring junior researchers. The position offers access to state-of-the-art facilities and opportunities for professional development within a collaborative research environment.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: The School of Architecture seeks Research Assistant(s) or Junior Research Assistant(s) to support architectural and multidisciplinary research projects in Hong Kong on a one-year renewable contract. Candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree or higher in Architecture, Landscape Architecture, Design, or related fields, with proficiency in English and Chinese and technical competency in AutoCAD, Photoshop, InDesign, Sketchup, and Microsoft Office suite. Responsibilities include project execution and administration, architectural research and design production, report preparation, field investigations, and stakeholder engagement across interdisciplinary teams with humanities and life sciences focus. Applicants must demonstrate strong communication, organisational, and teamwork skills and submit a statement of interest, CV, and design portfolio.

title: Research Assistant
department: Centre for Clinical Research and Biostatistics
description: This Research Assistant position supports the Centre for Clinical Research and Biostatistics in coordinating clinical research and data management projects with both local and international scope, particularly focused on pharmaceutical and medical device development. The role requires a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Science, strong organisational and project management skills, and proficiency in English, with preferred knowledge of statistics and programming. Key responsibilities include administrative project support, assistance with clinical research initiatives, involvement in protocol and case report form development, data management, and multi-centre study coordination. The position is offered on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow / Research Associate
description: This postdoctoral position in the Multimedia Communications Laboratory seeks a researcher with a PhD in a relevant discipline and demonstrated research experience to conduct independent research in mobile networking, Internet protocols, and machine learning. The role combines research responsibilities with co-supervision of graduate and undergraduate students. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract with the possibility of renewal based on mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on investigating gene regulation in skeletal muscle stem cells, muscle regeneration, and aging within an orthopaedic research group. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biology, Biomedical Science, Bioinformatics, or related fields obtained within the last five years, with a strong publication record and demonstrated ability to work independently and lead others. Responsibilities include conducting experiments and computational analyses, interpreting research findings, contributing to grant proposals and manuscripts, and mentoring junior group members. The role offers a one-year renewable contract.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s) / Research Associate(s)
description: This position seeks postdoctoral fellows or research associates to conduct interdisciplinary research on brain structure and function under Dr. Sadia Shakil in a top-ranked biomedical engineering department. Candidates must hold a PhD in a relevant field (or a Master's degree plus four years of work experience) from a recognized institution, with strong foundations in mathematics, statistics, programming (MATLAB/Python), neuroimaging techniques (MRI/fMRI/EEG), and machine learning/AI. The role emphasizes analysis and model development combining multiple disciplines, and applicants should demonstrate strong English communication and technical writing abilities. Positions are available immediately on renewable one-year contracts.

title: Research Assistant(s)
description: The position is for a Research Assistant supporting a large-scale AI education initiative aimed at integrating artificial intelligence into Hong Kong secondary school curricula. Core responsibilities include developing bilingual educational materials, creating hands-on courseware using programming languages and AI frameworks, and delivering training to schools and students. Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, AI, Data Science or related fields, with strong English communication skills; desirable qualifications include AI expertise, Python proficiency, teaching experience, and Chinese language abilities. The role is offered initially on a two-year renewable contract basis.

title: Research Assistant
description: A Research Assistant is sought for clinical research in orthopaedics, requiring a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Biomedical Sciences, Biology, or related fields, along with proven research experience and fluency in English and Cantonese. The role involves managing clinical trials, handling tissue samples, obtaining research ethics approvals, coordinating projects, and preparing manuscripts, with prior experience in blood-taking and clinical data analysis being advantageous. The position is based at a hospital and focuses on sports medicine and osteoarthritis research, requiring independent work capability and collaborative team engagement. The appointment is initially a one-year contract, renewable based on performance.

title: Senior Research Assistant(s) (External Fund) / Research Assistant(s)
description: The School of Architecture seeks two research positions to join a team studying Hong Kong's urban spaces using advanced data-driven methods, including public space activity patterns, public housing improvements, and elderly people's needs in dense urban areas. Applicants must hold a Master's degree in Architecture, Urban Design, Urban Analytics, or Computer Science with four years of relevant experience, and demonstrate strong proficiency in 3D modelling (Rhino3D, Grasshopper, Python) and written English. The Senior Research Assistant role involves developing Computer Vision analysis methods, managing data collection and processing, and conducting computational analysis in collaboration with the Principal Investigator, while the Research Assistant position focuses on administrative support, digital modelling, and spatial analysis of housing estates. The positions offer career development opportunities including research skills training, co-authored publications, and exposure to international conferences and teaching.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
department: Centre for Cardiovascular Genomics and Medicine
description: This postdoctoral position involves conducting basic and clinical research in cardiovascular diseases with a focus on translational discoveries and precision medicine development within a dedicated genomics centre. The successful candidate will hold a PhD in biomedical science, bioinformatics, statistics, or related fields, with proficiency in both English and Cantonese, and ideally possess prior experience in genetics, genomics, statistics, or bioinformatics. The role requires strong teamwork and responsibility, and is initially offered on a one-year contract basis with potential renewal depending on performance. The position is distinctive for its emphasis on bridging research discoveries with clinical applications in cardiovascular medicine.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This full-time postdoctoral position within a translation research centre focuses on Chinese translation history, requiring a PhD in this field or a related discipline and a demonstrated track record of research publication. Primary responsibilities include producing articles for international publications, supporting the centre director's research initiatives, helping to organize academic events, and building collaborative networks within the discipline. Candidates must demonstrate capacity for multidisciplinary work and provide a resume, cover letter, academic credentials, and at least one reference letter. The initial one-year contract position is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement, with the start date flexible.

title: Research Fellow
description: This Research Fellow position in the Department of Electronic Engineering seeks a highly experienced researcher specializing in antenna engineering, microwave engineering, radar systems, or synthetic aperture radars, with a Ph.D. and at least eight years of postqualification experience in these fields. The successful candidate will conduct independent, top-tier research under professorial supervision, contribute to postgraduate student mentoring, and demonstrate excellence through publications and a strong academic record. Key requirements include fluency in English, organizational skills, self-motivation, and proven expertise preferably in antenna engineering or synthetic aperture radar systems. The role is offered initially on a one-year contract renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Joint CUHK-NTEC Clinical Research Management Office
description: This Research Assistant position supports clinical research operations at a hospital research centre, requiring a bachelor's degree in biological science or health-related fields and fluent English and Chinese communication skills. Key responsibilities include organizing clinical research projects, coordinating studies, collecting and managing clinical data, performing site monitoring and start-up activities, and providing general administrative support. The role particularly values strong interpersonal skills, responsibility, self-motivation, and MS Office proficiency, with clinical research experience preferred but not mandatory. The appointment is a one-year renewable contract position.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Postdoctoral fellows are sought to join a pharmacy research team, developing natural language processing algorithms to extract content from Chinese and English articles. Candidates must hold a PhD in Pharmacy, Computer Science, or related fields from a reputable institution, with proficiency in at least one programming language and fluent English and Chinese communication skills. Key responsibilities include programming algorithm implementation, leading research projects, securing external funding, training team members, and preparing research documentation. The position offers a one-year renewable contract contingent on performance.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
department: Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences
description: The Department of Ophthalmology and Visual Sciences seeks postdoctoral fellows to support clinical and research programs across diverse ophthalmic specialties, including retinal diseases, glaucoma, ocular cancers, and genetic eye conditions. Candidates must hold a PhD in Biological Sciences (preferably ophthalmology or cancer research), demonstrate proficiency in molecular and cell biology techniques, and show strong independent and collaborative research abilities. Key responsibilities include coordinating research projects, analyzing data, preparing manuscripts and grant applications, and training junior staff. The position is offered on a one to two-year renewable contract, with preference given to applicants experienced in next-generation sequencing.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
department: Centre for University and School Partnership
description: This Research Assistant position supports a university center focused on school partnerships, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Social Sciences or education with strong bilingual academic writing skills (English and Chinese) and knowledge of Hong Kong's educational context. The role involves assisting with research and evaluation of centre projects, compiling reports and deliverables, and providing project support, with opportunities for those with prior experience in development programme research or evaluation. Applicants must demonstrate self-motivation, organizational ability, and strong interpersonal skills, though less experienced candidates may be considered for a junior-level appointment. The position is initially offered on a one-year renewable contract basis.

title: Research Assistant(s) / Junior Research Assistant(s)
description: Applicants with a Bachelor's degree in Biology, Environmental Science, Marine Science, or related fields (or an Associate degree for Junior positions) are sought for this role, which prioritizes fluency in spoken and written Chinese and English, strong communication skills, and demonstrated interest in marine and coral conservation. The position involves organizing and delivering educational programs for secondary schools, including conservation talks and workshops, managing stakeholder relationships, coordinating educational materials, overseeing program budgets, and maintaining social media presence. Experience in educational delivery and scuba diving certification are preferred, and candidates should be comfortable with occasional weekend work and outdoor activities. The appointment is offered on a one-year contract basis renewable by mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow(s)
description: Multiple postdoctoral positions are available to conduct research on smart materials and devices, with focus areas including fabrication, thermal management for energy efficiency, advanced sensing and camouflage technologies, flexible electronics, and soft grippers. Candidates must hold a PhD from a top 100 institution in STEM fields, with background in Materials Science/Engineering, Energy, Electronic Engineering, or related physical sciences, alongside strong analytical and communication skills, demonstrated academic excellence, and hands-on experience in materials and device fabrication. Appointments are initially for one year on a contract basis, renewable by mutual agreement. Applications should include a CV, transcripts, representative publications, and demonstration projects.

title: Research Assistant
department: Department of Sports Science and Physical Education
description: This full-time research assistant position requires a Bachelor's degree or higher in Physical Education, Sports Science, Exercise Science, Rehabilitation, Public Health, or related fields, along with fluency in written and spoken English and Chinese. The role involves recruiting school participants, collecting and analyzing research data, organizing and delivering physical activity interventions and workshops, and supporting other departmental tasks. Strong communication skills and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively are essential, with prior experience working with children and youth with special educational needs being advantageous. The position is offered initially on a one-year contract, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Full-time/Part-time Research Associate
department: Hong Kong Institute of Asia-Pacific Studies
description: This role involves supervising a team of telephone interviewers at a survey research laboratory with 60 workstations, overseeing data collection operations during evening shifts (2:30–10:30 pm for full-time or 6:30–10:30 pm for part-time positions). Candidates must hold a Master's degree, possess at least ten years of relevant post-qualification experience, demonstrate fluency in Cantonese and spoken English, and exhibit strong problem-solving and organisational abilities, with survey management experience preferred. The position is initially offered on a one-year contract basis, renewable based on performance and mutual agreement, and supports the laboratory's mission of providing professional survey services to researchers and conducting public opinion polls.

title: Research Assistant
description: A Research Assistant position is available to support investigations into cardiomyocyte differentiation, heart development, and regeneration using cultured mammalian cells, zebrafish, and mouse models, alongside adeno-associated virus techniques. The successful candidate will require a Bachelor's degree in Biomedical Sciences or a related field, with foundational experience in molecular and cellular biology and biochemistry, alongside proficiency in standard laboratory equipment and techniques. Desirable qualifications include recent hands-on laboratory experience, molecular biology competency, CRISPR/Cas9 knowledge, and zebrafish handling skills. The role is offered on a one-year contract basis, commencing immediately, with renewal contingent upon performance and mutual agreement.

title: Postdoctoral Fellow
description: This postdoctoral position focuses on investigating cardiomyocyte differentiation, heart development, and heart regeneration using cultured mammalian cells, zebrafish, and mouse models, with applications of adeno-associated viruses. Candidates must hold a Ph.D. in Biomedical Sciences or related fields and demonstrate strong laboratory experience in molecular and cellular biology, with desirable expertise in CRISPR/Cas9, mouse surgery, and zebrafish handling. Responsibilities include designing and executing research projects, presenting findings at conferences, writing manuscripts, and receiving mentorship in grant application writing. The initial one-year contract is renewable based on performance and mutual agreement.

title: Research Assistant I/II (R7063) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: The position involves assisting a Principal Investigator with research projects focused on lifestyle modification and disease self-management in areas including diabetes, cognitive impairment, and obesity. Key responsibilities include coordinating participant recruitment, developing and delivering instruments, conducting intervention and in-depth interviews, and performing administrative tasks. Candidates require a Bachelor's degree (for Research Assistant II) or Master's degree (for Research Assistant I) in nursing, social sciences, psychology, statistics, or healthcare-related fields, preferably with counselling experience; fresh graduates are welcome and on-the-job training in research methods and counselling will be provided. The position offers competitive remuneration, including salary and gratuity, alongside benefits such as annual leave, medical and dental coverage, and professional development sponsorship.

title: Technical Officer (Digital Construction Recording Project) (S&T)
description: This position supports a digital documentation project that captures construction activities on campus through photography, video, and 360-degree imaging for educational purposes. The Technical Officer will coordinate with contractors and site teams to execute recording schedules and catalogue the resulting data to make it accessible for teaching. The role requires a degree or higher diploma in construction-related fields such as Civil Engineering, Building Studies, Construction Management, or Surveying, and is distinctive in bridging construction industry knowledge with academic support by transforming site records into classroom materials.

title: Project Manager (Digital Construction Recording Project) (S&T)
description: This position manages a digital documentation initiative for a campus construction project, transforming the build into an educational resource by recording critical construction phases and capturing metrics across multiple disciplines. The role requires collaboration with construction teams and academic staff to develop recording schedules while supervising technical equipment and infrastructure, and coordinating with external consultants and contractors to minimize disruption. Key requirements include project management expertise, technical proficiency, and ability to balance comprehensive documentation with operational constraints. The role is distinctive in bridging construction management and academic teaching through innovative digital recording technology.

title: Assistant Accounting Officer (General Ledger) (FO)
description: The position involves preparing journal entries and supporting financial schedules, assisting in developing standard operating procedures for general ledger workflows, and performing other assigned duties within the Finance Office. Candidates must have at least five HKDSE Level 2 subjects (including English, Chinese, and Mathematics) and preferably three years of accounting experience, with Oracle Finance system knowledge being advantageous. The role offers competitive remuneration including base salary, end-of-contract gratuity, discretionary incentive payments, and comprehensive benefits such as annual leave, professional development sponsorships, medical and dental coverage, and life insurance.

title: Development and Alumni Affairs Officer (DAAO)
description: This role supports fundraising and alumni engagement at an applied sciences university, involving donor cultivation activities, alumni communications management, and database administration. The ideal candidate requires a bachelor's degree, minimum two years of professional experience, and trilingual fluency in English, Cantonese, and Putonghua, along with strong organizational skills and digital marketing knowledge. The position emphasizes independent work, meticulous attention to detail, and the ability to juggle multiple projects, with occasional travel required. Distinctively, the role bridges the university's applied sciences focus with alumni and donor relations while supporting institutional growth into the Greater Bay Area.

title: Senior Manager (Human Resources) (HRO)
description: This Senior Manager position in the Human Resources Office oversees the development and implementation of HR strategies while leading a team delivering services across Schools and Offices, managing employee relations, complaints, and disciplinary matters. The role requires a bachelor's degree in Human Resources Management, Business Administration, or related field, alongside strong leadership, problem-solving, and analytical skills, with preference given to candidates with advanced qualifications. The position offers distinctive appeal through involvement in shaping HR functions within an institution experiencing significant growth and regional expansion into a major economic zone.

title: Student Affairs Manager (SAO)
description: This role manages the Student Affairs Office's First Year Experience Programme, serving as its primary architect to foster student engagement and smooth transitions into university life. The position requires driving continuous improvement across all SAO programmes through assessment frameworks, data-driven insights, and operational excellence while developing performance indicators to measure effectiveness. The manager will coordinate global learning opportunities by collaborating with academic departments and external partners to deliver international experiences. Additional responsibilities include supporting evidence-based practices among staff and performing duties assigned by the Director of Student Affairs.

title: Assistant Librarian I / II (Library Systems and Technology) (LIB)
description: This position oversees library technology infrastructure, including integrated library systems and digital discovery platforms, while developing strategic initiatives to enhance service delivery. The Assistant Librarian will establish systems policies, evaluate emerging technologies, and contribute to collection development and academic liaison work. The role requires a relevant qualification plus 2-6 years of post-qualification experience in academic library systems, with demonstrated technical expertise in integrated library management and digital platforms. The position uniquely bridges technical proficiency with service orientation to support institutional teaching, research, and community engagement.

title: Public Affairs Officer (Multimedia Design) (PAO)
description: This position creates diverse multimedia content—videos, graphics, motion graphics, and social media posts—to strengthen the institution's digital marketing across platforms including Facebook, Instagram, and Xiaohongshu. The role requires proficiency in video production, graphic design, and social media creation, alongside WordPress website maintenance and cross-team collaboration to ensure visually consistent, brand-aligned content. What distinguishes this role is its focus on applied multimedia design that directly drives institutional branding and community engagement, particularly as the organization expands into the Greater Bay Area, demanding awareness of emerging design trends and adaptability in a dynamic digital environment.

title: Research Associate (Digital Health) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This Research Associate position supports digital health initiatives focused on wearable devices, mobile applications, and health sensors to enable personalized patient care within a nursing and health sciences school. The role encompasses project management, development of AI-based predictive tools, construction of hardware and software platforms, team coordination, and data analysis including database design. The position requires a PhD in Electronic Engineering, Computer Science, Medical Engineering, or a related field, with demonstrated expertise in backend frameworks such as PHP, Node.js, or Python, and frontend technologies. What distinguishes this role is its unique intersection of advanced digital technologies and artificial intelligence directly applied to healthcare challenges, effectively bridging engineering expertise with clinical outcomes.

title: Assistant Finance Manager (Procurement) (FO)
description: This Assistant Finance Manager (Procurement) role supports the Finance Manager by leading procurement operations, managing inventory and fixed assets, and developing sourcing strategies to support organizational objectives. The position requires a degree in purchasing, supply management, or business administration plus at least three years of post-qualification experience in procurement, tendering, and vendor management, ideally from larger organizations. Essential competencies include strong planning and problem-solving skills, fluency in English and Chinese, and proficiency with Microsoft Office and procurement management systems. The role combines strategic procurement development with day-to-day operational management, including oversight of corporate insurance programs.

title: Research Assistant II (R6586) (N&HS)
department: School of Nursing and Health Sciences
description: This Research Assistant II position supports an exergaming research project focused on healthy aging, requiring a Bachelor's degree or higher in Psychology, Education Psychology, or a health-related field. Primary responsibilities include assisting with data collection and analysis, managing databases, preparing study materials, and instructing older adult participants while monitoring their engagement and well-being. Preferred qualifications include experience with fNIRS data collection and analysis, strong communication and problem-solving abilities, and prior research experience with older populations. The role offers competitive compensation including salary, gratuity, generous leave entitlements, professional development support, and comprehensive benefits.

title: Assistant Finance Manager (Payment) (FO)
description: This position involves supervising a payment operations team and overseeing accounts payable functions, including payment scheduling, reconciliations, and month-end closing processes. The successful candidate must possess a degree in Accountancy or a related field with at least 3 years of post-qualification experience in larger organizations, with professional accounting qualifications such as CPA strongly preferred. Key requirements include strong analytical and organizational skills, meticulous attention to detail, ability to manage competing deadlines under pressure, and fluency in English and Chinese. The role is distinctive for prioritizing process improvement and system automation to enhance departmental efficiency.

title: Research Assistant I/II (Project Ref. No.: CS/106/2412/RA) (S&T)
description: The position supports a research project developing a driving performance analysis system for driving tests, with primary responsibilities including frontend user interface implementation using modern frameworks and backend server logic/API development in collaboration with the engineering team. Candidates must hold a Master's degree or above in a computing-related discipline (Electronic Engineering, Computer Science, AI, Software Engineering, etc.) for Research Assistant I, or a Bachelor's degree for Research Assistant II in the same fields. Full-stack developer experience and a valid driving licence are advantageous but not required. The role offers competitive remuneration including base salary and end-of-contract gratuity.

title: Assistant Finance Manager (Financial Accounting) (FO)
description: This role requires a qualified accountant with a minimum of 4 years' post-qualification experience to manage the Financial Accounting team and oversee general accounting operations. Primary responsibilities include preparing consolidated financial statements and management accounts, managing externally-funded project reporting, and driving process improvements to enhance efficiency. The successful candidate must be self-motivated, well-organized, and capable of performing effectively under pressure, with strong communication and service-oriented capabilities. The position offers competitive compensation, professional development support, and comprehensive benefits including medical, dental, and life insurance coverage.

title: Assistant Industry Partnerships Manager (SAO)
